2724538897.Title: Is the server port options taken in account from the configuration file?
2724538897.Author: e-roux
2724538897.Body:
### Version

2.4.2

### Platform

Darwin

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

After bootstrapping a site with `deno run -A https://lume.land/init.ts` with the configuration:

```ts
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";

const site = lume({
  server: {
    port: 8000,
  },
});

export default site;
```

the site is accessible on port 3000 but not 8000

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

always

### What is the expected behavior?

```shell
Task serve deno task lume -s
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A - "-s"
Loading config file file:///private/tmp/lumi/_config.ts
🍾 Site built into ./_site
  0 files generated in 0.00 seconds
  Server started at:
  http://localhost:8000/ (local)
  http://192.168.178.30:8000/ (network)
```

### What do you see instead?

```shell
Task serve deno task lume -s
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A - "-s"
Loading config file file:///private/tmp/lumi/_config.ts
🍾 Site built into ./_site
  0 files generated in 0.00 seconds
  Server started at:
  http://localhost:3000/ (local)
  http://192.168.178.30:3000/ (network)
```

### Additional information

The port is taken in account when passed to the command line
2724538897.PlainText:
Is the server port options taken in account from the configuration file?

Version
2.4.2
Platform
Darwin
What steps will reproduce the bug?
After bootstrapping a site with deno run -A  with the configuration:
the site is accessible on port 3000 but not 8000
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
always
What is the expected behavior?
What do you see instead?
Additional information
The port is taken in account when passed to the command line

Comments.Body:
Sorry, this is a bug. Seems that the default url for server mode (`localhost:3000`) overrides the port. I'll fix it in the next version.
Meanwhile, you can use `--port` flag (`serve deno task lume -s --port=8000`)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: thanks for the quick reponse. Works with the flag passed to `deno task serve`
Comments.Author: e-roux

2746724732.Title: Skipped page (file content is empty) warning is not useful for type-only TypeScript files with esbuild
2746724732.Author: domenic
2746724732.Body:
### Version

2.4.3

### Platform

Windows

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

I have a TypeScript file which just centralizes some type definitions.

Whenever I build my Lume site, I get an unhelpful warning telling me that the corresponding `.js` file is empty and will be skipped. But this should not be a warning; this is expected.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Full repro:

**deno.json**

```json
{
  "imports": {
    "lume/": "https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.4.3/"
  },
  "tasks": {
    "lume": "echo \"import 'lume/cli.ts'\" | deno run -A -",
    "build": "deno task lume",
    "serve": "deno task lume -s"
  },
  "compilerOptions": {
    "types": [
      "lume/types.ts"
    ]
  }
}
```

**_config.ts**

```ts
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";
import esbuild from "lume/plugins/esbuild.ts";

const site = lume({
  src: "./src",
});

site.copy([".html"]);

site.use(esbuild({
  extensions: [".ts"],
  options: {
    bundle: false,
    minify: false,
    treeShaking: false,
    keepNames: false,
  },
}));

export default site;
```

**src/types.ts**

```ts
export type StringAlias = string;
```

**src/test.ts**

```ts
import { StringAlias } from "./types.ts";

const string: StringAlias = "Hello, world!";

document.body.innerHTML = string;
```

**src/index.html**

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<title>Test</title>

<script type="module" src="test.js"></script>
```

### What is the expected behavior?

When running `deno run build`, I'd get something like

```
$ deno run build
Task build deno task lume
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A -
Loading config file file:///C:/Users/d/OneDrive%20-%20domenic.me/Code/WIP/lume-test/_config.ts
🔥 /test.js <- /test.ts
🔥 /index.html <- /index.html
🍾 Site built into ./_site
  3 files generated in 0.03 seconds
```

### What do you see instead?

I get

```
$ deno run build
Task build deno task lume
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A -
Loading config file file:///C:/Users/d/OneDrive%20-%20domenic.me/Code/WIP/lume-test/_config.ts
WARN [Lume] Skipped page /types.js (file content is empty)
🔥 /test.js <- /test.ts
🔥 /index.html <- /index.html
🍾 Site built into ./_site
  3 files generated in 0.03 seconds
```

### Additional information

- It'd be ideal if this situation could be detected and handled without me having to manually annotate the files in `_config.ts`.

- It's a little strange the message says "Skipped page" because a JavaScript file is not a page.

- /cc @bryophyta who seems to have originally implemented this warning in https://github.com/lumeland/lume/pull/221.
2746724732.PlainText:
Skipped page (file content is empty) warning is not useful for type-only TypeScript files with esbuild

Version
2.4.3
Platform
Windows
What steps will reproduce the bug?
I have a TypeScript file which just centralizes some type definitions.
Whenever I build my Lume site, I get an unhelpful warning telling me that the corresponding .js file is empty and will be skipped. But this should not be a warning; this is expected.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Full repro:
deno.json
_config.ts
src/types.ts
src/test.ts
src/index.html
What is the expected behavior?
When running deno run build, I'd get something like
What do you see instead?
I get
Additional information
It'd be ideal if this situation could be detected and handled without me having to manually annotate the files in _config.ts.
It's a little strange the message says "Skipped page" because a JavaScript file is not a page.
/cc @bryophyta who seems to have originally implemented this warning in .

Comments.Body:
That's not a bug. Lume pages are not only html pages. [Learn about html pages and asset pages](https://lume.land/docs/core/concepts/#page-files). It's just the way to tell you that Lume won't save a file with no content.

I understand the issue but I don't know if it's easy to fix. It's like if you have a javascript file with all code commented (and removed by a minifier). The best way to do this is ignoring the file renaming it to `_types.ts` or in the _config.ts file adding the `site.ignore("types.ts")`.

Maybe, due it's only a file with types declarations, you can rename it to `types.d.ts`. I'm not sure if Lume ignored the `.d.ts` files but definitely it should. I can make Lume to ignore this extension by default. What do you think?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: I'd never used personally-written `.d.ts` files before, and [the documentation](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/declaration-files/templates/module-d-ts.html) doesn't make it seem like it would work. But I just tested renaming my file to use that extension, and it seems to work well! So I think this is a pretty good solution.
Comments.Author: domenic

2762314153.Title: esbuild plugin doesn't build when src is specified

And this index.md in both / and /docs:

Comments.Body:
Are your scripts inside `docs` folder?
Note also that you don't need to specify `entryPoints`. Lume automatically bundle all `ts` and `js` files found in the `src` folder. You can simply run `site.use(esbuild())` (without options) and it should work.

2763063044.Title: relative_urls plugin has trouble with trailing slashes in URLs
2763063044.Author: satgo1546
2763063044.Body:
### Version 2.4.3 ### Platform Linux (WSL), should also reproduce on other platforms ### What steps will reproduce the bug? In a directory with two files: - `_config.ts`: ```ts import lume from "lume/mod.ts"; import relativeUrls from "lume/plugins/relative_urls.ts"; const site = lume(); site.use(relativeUrls()); export default site; ``` - `test.md`: ```md --- url: /a.html --- [Go to index](/) ``` Run `lume`. ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Always reproducible. ### What is the expected behavior? The URL `/` should be translated to `.`, so that the link leads to `index.html`. ### What do you see instead? In `_site/a.html`, the URL `/` is translated to an empty string. The link leads to `a.html` itself. ```html

Go to index

``` ### Additional information There are many other cases where the plugin fails; the above is just an example. It is problematic even if `url` is not set explicitly: Trailing slashes in URLs are removed, but `prettyUrls` (on by default) requires them. While Lume dev server handles missing trailing slashes by redirection, [not all static hosting providers do](https://www.zachleat.com/web/trailing-slash/#results-table). The plugin uses `jsr:@std/path/posix`, a module intended for POSIX filesystems. In POSIX path operations, trailing slashes are ignored. But in URLs, they are significant.
2763063044.PlainText:
relative_urls plugin has trouble with trailing slashes in URLs

Version
2.4.3
Platform
Linux (WSL), should also reproduce on other platforms
What steps will reproduce the bug?
In a directory with two files:
_config.ts:
test.md:
Run lume.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Always reproducible.
What is the expected behavior?
The URL / should be translated to ., so that the link leads to index.html.
What do you see instead?
In _site/a.html, the URL / is translated to an empty string. The link leads to a.html itself.
Additional information
There are many other cases where the plugin fails; the above is just an example.
It is problematic even if url is not set explicitly: Trailing slashes in URLs are removed, but prettyUrls (on by default) requires them. While Lume dev server handles missing trailing slashes by redirection, not all static hosting providers do.
The plugin uses jsr:@std/path/posix, a module intended for POSIX filesystems. In POSIX path operations, trailing slashes are ignored. But in URLs, they are significant.

Comments.Body:
Thank you for reporting this!
I just commited a fix that will be available in the next Lume version.
If you don't want to wait to the next version, run `deno task lume upgrade --dev` to use the latest development version.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2773200405.Title: Relative Import Path of package dependencies throw error on build
2773200405.Author: tspears1
2773200405.Body:
### Version

2.4.3

### Platform

OSX

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

I'm using the `radix-ui` library for building a React client-side app on my site with esbuild to compile. I've tried adding these packages with ESM and NPM and in both cases, if the package has dependencies `lume-loader` throws an error like the following for `@radix-ui/react-dialog`:

`deno:file:///Users/user/Library/Caches/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/@radix-ui/react-dialog/1.1.4/dist/index.mjs:16:29:ERROR: [plugin: lume-loader] Relative import path "react-remove-scroll" not prefixed with / or ./ or ../ and not in import map from "https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.4.3/plugins/esbuild.ts"`

I even have those particularly directories ignored by LUME however it seems they're still being processed. I've gotten around it a few times by adding the package dependency to the import map, but its a tedious process and not always successful. Any suggestions?

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

_No response_

### What is the expected behavior?

Libraries to be imported without conflict.

### What do you see instead?

```bash
Loading config file file:///Users/user/Sites/project/_config.js
✘ [ERROR] Relative import path "react-remove-scroll" not prefixed with / or ./ or ../ and not in import map from "https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.4.3/plugins/esbuild.ts" [plugin lume-loader]

    deno:file:///Users/user/Library/Caches/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/@radix-ui/react-dialog/1.1.4/dist/index.mjs:16:29:
      16 │ import { RemoveScroll } from "react-remove-scroll";

_No response_

Comments.Body:
Yes, this is due a bug (or a behavior change, I don't know) of Deno.
I've reported to them here but didn't get any response yet: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/27436

I'll try to find another way to resolve npm dependencies in Deno.
Meanwhile, can you try importing the package from `esm.sh`? 

```js
import radix from "https://esm.sh/radix-ui";
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
That's very helpful to know! I'll keep an eye on that thread. (and thanks for the quick reply)

SO with ESM, if I mark it as external (`"@radix-ui/react-dialog": "https://esm.sh/v135/*@radix-ui/react-dialog@1.1.4",`), I get the same issue as with NPM.

If I dont mark it as external I keep getting errors like `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'useState')` which seems to be coming from a conflict of different version of React being used. (though for the life of me I can't tell where. everything is using React 18.3.1 yet React 19 keeps popping up in my deno.lock file.)
Comments.Author: tspears1

Comments.Body:
I suspect this bug won't be fixed anytime soon, because they didn't respond in 3 weeks.

I've trying to fix the plugin using the [import_map](https://github.com/denoland/import_map) package instead of relying on Deno's `import.meta.resolve()` function.

This has the benefit of defining a custom import map for the esbuild, independent of the `deno.json` file.

Can you try it now? It's available in the latest development version of Lume. You can upgrade it with `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.

If it doesn't work and you have a public repo with your code, I can take a look.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@oscarotero - I think that solved it, at least for ESM imports. (Still running into some issues with React conflicting, but I think those are probably ME problems lol and nothing to do with Lume.)

There may still be an issue with `npm` when getting subfolders though. With `esm` you can solve it with a second import map entry. So for `import ReactDOMServer from "react-dom/server"` you need the following.
```
"react-dom": "https://esm.sh/react-dom@18.3.1",
"react-dom/": "https://esm.sh/react-dom@18.3.1/",
```
Which if you use the ESM CLI, it actually adds both automatically.
If you try to do that with the npm, you run into the same problem still.
Comments.Author: tspears1

Comments.Body:
If you want to take a look you can see the repo here: https://github.com/tspears1/projects.bu.edu
I just pushed a new branch `update-lume` with the new dev version.
Comments.Author: tspears1

Comments.Body:
Ok, I've commited new changes to Lume (you can run `deno task lume upgrade --dev` again, to fetch the latest version).

- npm specifiers don't need to add the trailing slash. Only http specifiers need that. For example this works:
  ```
  "react-dom": "npm:react-dom@18.3.1"
  ```
  (no need to add `"react-dom/"`)
- Now, changing all dependencies to `npm` makes the build to work and there are no errors. I have the following:
   ```json
   "@radix-ui": "npm:@radix-ui@1.0.1",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "npm:@radix-ui/react-dialog@1.1.4",
    "@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu": "npm:@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu@2.1.4",
    "@radix-ui/react-separator": "npm:@radix-ui/react-separator@1.1.1",
    "@radix-ui/react-slot": "npm:@radix-ui/react-slot@1.1.1",
    "@radix-ui/react-tooltip": "npm:@radix-ui/react-tooltip@1.1.6",
    "classnames": "npm:classnames@2.5.1",
    "react-dom": "npm:react-dom@18.3.1",
    "react-inlinesvg": "npm:react-inlinesvg@4.1.5",
    "react": "npm:react@18.3.1"
   ```
- But the app doesn't work (there are errors in the browser). Some components work fine, others not. Maybe the error is caused by some dependency that is not correctly bundled by esm.sh. I don't know.
- Note that esm [accept some configuration](https://lume.land/plugins/esbuild/#esm). For example, you can configure the dependencies of some packages, and this could fix errors like having different react versions. 
- I can't help more at this point. If you discover the dependency that is causing the error and there is something that I can do in Lume, please let me know.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Thanks @oscarotero - this is all extremely helpful and I can take it from here. 
The fix works great for getting the build to work. I'll chip away at the React issue - I'll let you know if I find anything related to Lume.

Appreciate all your help - loving Lume and the Lume CMS!
Comments.Author: tspears1

Comments.Body:
I've been playing with your repo a bit more and discover that esm.sh mixes React 18 and 19.
It seems to be a bug on esm.sh and i've opened an issue here: https://github.com/esm-dev/esm.sh/issues/994

Maybe this is why JS breaks in the browser.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Ah! This is what I suspected but wasn't able to prove yet. Yeah I think that's right. React 19 keeps popping up in my `deno.lock`. Thanks for opening that ticket - I'll keep an eye out.
Comments.Author: tspears1

Comments.Body: I'm closing this since Lume 3 uses the official [deno loader](https://jsr.io/@deno/loader) dependency and it works great with npm packages.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2788443599.Title: Watcher ignore configuration is confusing
2788443599.Author: sagikazarmark
2788443599.Body:
### Version

2.5.0

### Platform

darwin

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. Create a directory you want to ignore (eg. `.devenv`)
2. Follow the [documentation](https://lume.land/docs/configuration/config-file/#watcher-options) to ignore it from the file watcher
3. The directory doesn't get ignored (in my case it leads to some issues, see below)

For reference, this is the initial configuration I tried:

```ts
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";

const site = lume({
    watcher: {
        ignore: [
            ".devenv",
            ".direnv",

            "./.devenv",
            "./.direnv",
        ],
    },
});

site.ignore(".devenv", ".direnv");

export default site;
```

To make things even more confusing, the watcher ignore config and the site ignore config does not seem to be in sync (which was my initial assumption).

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

_No response_

### What is the expected behavior?

The file watcher should ignore said directories.

### What do you see instead?

The file watcher entering the directories I want to exclude have two consequences in my case:

These directories contain a lot of files which means starting Lume takes a lot of time.

It also failed to start due to a broken link in those directories:

```
error: Uncaught (in worker "") (in promise) NotFound: No such file or directory (os error 2): stat '/Users/mark/Projects/sagikazarmark/sagikazarmark.com/.direnv/flake-inputs/8p8giar71b6aqddqkjckzhirwvjnf02x-source/tests/functional/lang/symlink-resolution/broken'
    const info = Deno.statSync(src);
                      ^
    at Object.statSync (ext:deno_fs/30_fs.js:415:3)
    at FS.#walkLink (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:167:23)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:141:23)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
    at FS.#walkLink (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:181:19)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:141:23)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
error: Uncaught (in promise) Error: Unhandled error in child worker.
    at Worker.#pollControl (ext:runtime/11_workers.js:204:19)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:175:7)
```

### Additional information

After some debugging I managed to get the configuration work:

```ts
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";

const site = lume({
    // src: "src",
    watcher: {
        ignore: [
            "/.devenv",
            "/.direnv",
        ],
    },
});

site.ignore(".devenv", ".direnv");

export default site;
```

Adding a leading slash to the path seems to have done the trick. There is no mention of that in the configuration though.

---

I use Nix with direnv and devenv to manage Deno, hence those two directories.

I also figured out I could just move the source directory somewhere else.
2788443599.PlainText:
Watcher ignore configuration is confusing

Version
2.5.0
Platform
darwin
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Create a directory you want to ignore (eg. .devenv)
Follow the documentation to ignore it from the file watcher
The directory doesn't get ignored (in my case it leads to some issues, see below)
For reference, this is the initial configuration I tried:
To make things even more confusing, the watcher ignore config and the site ignore config does not seem to be in sync (which was my initial assumption).
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
No response
What is the expected behavior?
The file watcher should ignore said directories.
What do you see instead?
The file watcher entering the directories I want to exclude have two consequences in my case:
These directories contain a lot of files which means starting Lume takes a lot of time.
It also failed to start due to a broken link in those directories:
Additional information
After some debugging I managed to get the configuration work:
Adding a leading slash to the path seems to have done the trick. There is no mention of that in the configuration though.
I use Nix with direnv and devenv to manage Deno, hence those two directories.
I also figured out I could just move the source directory somewhere else.

Comments.Body:
The `site.ignore()` ignores the src files to create pages, but these files are scanned because they can be used by any page. For example the `_includes` page is ignored (because it starts with `_` but it's the same if it were ignored with `site.ignore()`), but pages can include templates or use layouts from this folder.

The watcher ignore only instruct to don't trigger a new rebuild if some files have been changed. For default, the `_site` folder is ignored by the watcher (to avoid infinite loop) but the `_includes` folder isn't (even ignored by `site`, it's not ignored by the watcher).

What you want is a way to ignore completely the folder for both watcher and site. This isn't implemented for now (it's a good idea, I will do).

And regarding the leading slash, that's fair. It must be added automatically by Lume if it's missing. I'll fix it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2793926070.Title: TailwindCSS collides with postcss import
2793926070.Author: sagikazarmark
2793926070.PlainText:
TailwindCSS collides with postcss import

Version
2.5.0
Platform
darwin
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Configure Lume to use TailwindCSS:
Add tailwind directives and a local import to your CSS file:
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
No response
What is the expected behavior?
The custom CSS should be inlined.
What do you see instead?
The custom CSS doesn't get inlined.
Additional information
Doing the import first resolves the problem:
I wonder if it's a Lume or a TailwindCSS issue. I'm not sure to be honest. I could very well have missed something, but it's weird that moving the import to the top makes the issue go away.

Comments.Body:
By specs, `@import` must be before all other rules in the CSS file ([more info](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@import#description))

The [postcss-import](https://github.com/postcss/postcss-import) plugin used to inline the CSS files follows the specs. I think the plugin logs in the console an error when a `@import` not following the specs is found.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Ah, my bad then. I can't see any logs, though.
Comments.Author: sagikazarmark

Comments.Body: Closing as it works as intended.

2796641069.Title: Install all dependencies for local development
2796641069.Author: 9999years
2796641069.Body:
Hello, is there a way to install all dependencies for hacking on Lume, including for plugins? `deno install` doesn't seem to do anything. `deno task test` works, but I'm not sure how to install dependencies _without_ running tests.

Either way, this should be listed in `CONTRIBUTING.md`, as it's needed to get the project set up with the Deno language server!
2796641069.PlainText:
Install all dependencies for local development

Hello, is there a way to install all dependencies for hacking on Lume, including for plugins? deno install doesn't seem to do anything. deno task test works, but I'm not sure how to install dependencies without running tests.
Either way, this should be listed in CONTRIBUTING.md, as it's needed to get the project set up with the Deno language server!

Comments.Body:
For development, you can clone the lume repo locally. Then, in your project, map the lume specifier to your local copy:

```diff
{
    "imports": {
-       "lume/": "https://deno.land/x/lume@2.5.0/",
+       "lume/": "../lume/",
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2828120437.Title: Override SASS options.importer
2828120437.Author: tdillon
2828120437.Body:
Does the Lume SASS plugin allow the `importer` option to be overridden?  From the code, it doesn't appear possible since the options object is destructured before the default `importer` is defined.  Is that by design, or should the options destructuring occur later?

https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/60892e9551bb2936d3d1637f43d8c5e04c42644c/plugins/sass.ts#L73

I'm asking because I'm trying to use https://github.com/uswds/uswds in a Lume project and it doesn't seem like the Lume SASS plugin can handle the paths as defined by the USWDS.  Any help would be much appreciated.
2828120437.PlainText:
Override SASS options.importer

Does the Lume SASS plugin allow the importer option to be overridden?  From the code, it doesn't appear possible since the options object is destructured before the default importer is defined.  Is that by design, or should the options destructuring occur later?
I'm asking because I'm trying to use  in a Lume project and it doesn't seem like the Lume SASS plugin can handle the paths as defined by the USWDS.  Any help would be much appreciated.

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
It might.  I'd be happy to test anything out if you'd like.


My current attempt is:

```typescript
site.use(sass({includes: "node_modules/@uswds/uswds/packages/"}))
```

This only works for a few imports.  Eventually it errors based on which type of path USWDS uses in their `@use` statements.

For example, this file https://github.com/uswds/uswds/blob/develop/packages/uswds-core/src/styles/tokens/color/shortcodes-color-system.scss has 2 `@use` statements.

```sass
# Works with Lume
@use "../../settings" as *;

# Errors with Lume
# Looks for <repo-root>/node_modules/@uswds/uswds/packages/uswds-core/src/styles/tokens/color/uswds-core/src/styles/function/color/get-system-color
@use "uswds-core/src/styles/functions/color/get-system-color" as *;
```

I assumed I (or someone) needed to implement a "[Node package importer](https://sass-lang.com/blog/announcing-pkg-importers/)" for Lume to make this work, but if you can handle it with an includes map that would be just as good.

Thank you very much.
Comments.Author: tdillon
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I've been trying to modify the plugin to support uswds and similar packages but I can't make it to work. 

According to the docs, seems that node imports must be prefixed with `pkg`. For example `pkg:uswds`


I've also tried this:

```js
site.use(sass({includes: "node_modules/@uswds/uswds/packages/"}))
```

As you said, this works only for some imports, but Sass resolves automatically some urls before passing to the importer. For example the code `@use uswds-core` in the file `file:///project/example.sass` is passed to the importer as `file:///project/uswds-core`, instead of simply `uswds-core`, so it's not possible to know if the original import is relative (`./uswds-core`) or not.
I'll keep investigating.
Comments.Author: oscarotero
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Setting `loadPaths` is working for me (at least in the simple POC I have).

Thank you very much for the quick turnaround.

FYI: I did not test adding an importer.

2841614023.Title: Open Graph locale format set by Metas plugin is incorrect
2841614023.Author: yeskunall
2841614023.Body:
### Version

v2.5.1

### Platform

macOS 13.7.1

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. Set a `lang` (say, in `_data.yml`)
2. Use the `metas` plugin

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

If you set a `lang`, then it is [copied](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/main/plugins/metas.ts#L113) as-is into `og:locale`.

### What is the expected behavior?

The locale set in `og:locale` should be of the format `language_TERRITORY`. This can be confirmed by visiting the [spec](https://ogp.me/#optional).

### What do you see instead?

The `locale` is the same as the `lang` attribute.

### Additional information

_No response_
2841614023.PlainText:
Open Graph locale format set by Metas plugin is incorrect

Version
v2.5.1
Platform
macOS 13.7.1
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Set a lang (say, in _data.yml)
Use the metas plugin
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
If you set a lang, then it is copied as-is into og:locale.
What is the expected behavior?
The locale set in og:locale should be of the format language_TERRITORY. This can be confirmed by visiting the spec.
What do you see instead?
The locale is the same as the lang attribute.
Additional information
No response

Comments.Body:
That's a fair point, but I don't know how to address it, because it conflicts with other plugins using `lang` variable (multilanguage plugin, for instance). The requirement of including always the territory is a bit annoying because there are many territories using the same language. If you have versions in `en_US` and `en_UK`, it make sense, but if you only have a single version of english, which territory should be used? And Spanish, Portuguesse and many languages have the same problem.

Any ideas?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hey, thanks for the well-thought response. I’ll try my best to do the same.
> If you have versions in en_US and en_UK, it make sense, but if you only have a single version of english, which territory should be used? [RFC5646](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5646) requires hyphens but the Open Graph protocol (OGP) uses underscores. If we go by the RFC, then the only **required** part by the `lang` attribute is the 2 (or 3) [language subtag](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/lang#language_subtag). Based on that, if you do not want to specify a regional dialect, then you should simply pass `en`, or `es`, or `gl`. In setting a `lang` with a [region subtag](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/lang#region_subtag), I am saying this document must be parsed as if it was English as spoken in Canada (`en-CA`). But AFAIK, it doesn’t really make a difference to the browser. Maybe it does make a difference if you’re using assistive tech like a screenreader? I cannot confirm this myself. --- Can we do something like this? `ogLocale = (page.lang || site.lang || "en_US").replace("-", "_")`? If a user passes a `lang` that is valid according to the RFC (en-CA), then it will be written as `en_CA` for `og:locale`, otherwise it will be left as-is (which is the case today). The downside will be that if the `lang` is not of the format `language_TERRITORY`, then it will be marked as invalid according to OGP, which is already the case today. The above suggestion is just pseudo-code, but if you’re OK with the change, I can make a PR.
Comments.Author: yeskunall

Comments.Body:
> Can we do something like this? `ogLocale = (page.lang || site.lang || "en_US").replace("-", "_")`?

The ogLocale is not detected automatically from the page. Metas plugin get the values from the `metas` object. So you can have the language page defined in two places: the `lang` variable and the `metas.lang` variable.

```yml
lang: en
metas:
  lang: en-US
```

The `lang` variable can be used in your templates, or even the multiligual pages (for urls like `/en/whatever/`) and the `metas.lang` variable is used by metas plugin to create the `og:locale` tag.

To use the full language in both places, you can create an alias:

```yml
lang: en-US
metas:
  lang: =lang
```

This indicates that the value of `lang` is the same value of `metas.lang`. So I'm okay to make the conversion from `en-US` to `en_US` to meet the OG specifications.

You're very welcome to work on a PR, I think this replacement must be done here: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/main/plugins/metas.ts#L100
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2860690296.Title: RSS (Atom) feeds are not parsed as valid XML when using the `feed` plugin
2860690296.Author: yeskunall
2860690296.Body:
### Version

v2.5.1

### Platform

macOS 13.7.1

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. Use the [feed](https://lume.land/plugins/feed/) plugin to generate an RSS feed

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

> How often does it reproduce?

Always. Tested on Brave Browser.

The required condition is to use the [feed](https://lume.land/plugins/feed/) plugin as stated above.

### What is the expected behavior?

The feed should be parsed as valid XML.

### What do you see instead?

It is not parsed as valid XML. As a result, Brave Browser—and I believe all other browsers—render the feed as a regular text file, which is not ideal if you want to work with the XML file in the browser.

### Additional information

The `application/rss+xml` media type by the RSS Advisory Group [never took off](https://www.rssboard.org/rss-mime-type-application.txt). Instead, most modern browsers use the media types standardized in [RFC7303](https://www.iana.org/go/rfc7303), which are:

- `application/xml`
- `application/xml-external-parsed-entity`, and
- `application/xml-dtd`

I think Lume should use the first one on the list and perhaps fallback to `text/xml` (as an alias, as suggested in RFC7303). I tested both `application/xml` and `text/xml` by modifying the response headers in Chrome DevTools and it works as expected.

LMK if you need more info!

---

[IANA Media Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml)
[StackOverflow discussion from 2009](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/595616/what-is-the-correct-mime-type-to-use-for-an-rss-feed)
2860690296.PlainText:
RSS (Atom) feeds are not parsed as valid XML when using the feed plugin

Version
v2.5.1
Platform
macOS 13.7.1
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Use the feed plugin to generate an RSS feed
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
How often does it reproduce?
Always. Tested on Brave Browser.
The required condition is to use the feed plugin as stated above.
What is the expected behavior?
The feed should be parsed as valid XML.
What do you see instead?
It is not parsed as valid XML. As a result, Brave Browser—and I believe all other browsers—render the feed as a regular text file, which is not ideal if you want to work with the XML file in the browser.
Additional information
The application/rss+xml media type by the RSS Advisory Group never took off. Instead, most modern browsers use the media types standardized in RFC7303, which are:
application/xml
application/xml-external-parsed-entity, and
application/xml-dtd
I think Lume should use the first one on the list and perhaps fallback to text/xml (as an alias, as suggested in RFC7303). I tested both application/xml and text/xml by modifying the response headers in Chrome DevTools and it works as expected.
LMK if you need more info!
IANA Media Types
StackOverflow discussion from 2009

Comments.Body:
Ok, to clarify: the issue is the value of the content type serving the `.rss` file, right?

This content type is returned by `@std/media-types` library (https://jsr.io/@std/media-types/1.1.0/vendor/db.ts). I can fix it temporarily in Lume but the ideal solution would be to fix it in the origin of the problem.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> Ok, to clarify: the issue is the value of the content type serving the .rss file, right?

Correct.

> I can fix it temporarily in Lume but the ideal solution would be to fix it in the origin of the problem.

Absolutely correct. I will open an issue with the Deno guys, too. I just wanted to bring this to your attention. Thanks as always, Óscar!
Comments.Author: yeskunall

Comments.Body:
Hey, just wanted to drop by to give some more info:

`@std/media-types` depends on [`jshttp/mime-db`](https://github.com/jshttp/mime-db), which actually pulls the non-standard `application/rss+xml` from [Apache common media types](https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/httpd/httpd/trunk/docs/conf/mime.types). I will open an issue in the `mime-db` repo asking them to remove it. If it does, great, but it is unlikely it will be.
Comments.Author: yeskunall

Comments.Body:
If they don't fix them, maybe Deno's std package can make an exception for this.

Anyway, I just fixed it (https://github.com/lumeland/lume/commit/5ea3f2a70bc022f703e8d5b0349b6491c001afb9)
Can you try it and confirm if it works fine now?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hey Óscar, I didn’t have time to test it when you pinged me. Just did, and it works as expected. Thanks so much for the quick turnaround on this. 💖

I’m @import-ing the local file relative to the _includes folder. What do you see instead? No bundling. No minification.
Screenshot of browser console logs
Additional information Here’s the relevant parts of my _config.ts: Output of tree source: PS: Maybe I’m not using the plugin correctly, in which case thanks for pointing me in the right direction!

Comments.Body:
The problem is in `site.copy("/styles.css")`.
Copied files are not processed. If you remove this line, it should work.

This is the expected behavior but many people reported this as a bug, and it willl be fixed in Lume 3. You can see more details in the release post that I'm working on: https://github.com/lumeland/blog/blob/main/posts/lume-3.md
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> Copied files are not processed. If you remove this line, it should work.

Totally forgot about this. A massive oversight on my part. Thank you as always, Óscar!

FWIW, I also had to drop the relative path and simply use `reset.css` (and not `./reset.css`) or I got this error:

```sh
error: Uncaught (in worker "") (in promise) Error: No result
    at napi_create_error (file:///Users/kimchi/.cache/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/napi-wasm/1.1.3/index.mjs:959:15)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/0362caf2:1:270635)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/0362caf2:1:124704)
    at Object.bundle (file:///Users/kimchi/.cache/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/napi-wasm/1.1.3/index.mjs:265:19)
    at bundleAsync (file:///Users/kimchi/.cache/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/lightningcss-wasm/1.29.1/async.mjs:68:21)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:177:7)
    at async lightningCSSBundler (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.2/plugins/lightningcss.ts:144:24)
    at async Processors.run (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.2/core/processors.ts:32:7)
    at async Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.2/core/site.ts:702:5)
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.2/core/site.ts:550:9)
error: Uncaught (in promise) Error: Unhandled error in child worker.
    at Worker.#pollControl (ext:runtime/11_workers.js:204:19)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:177:7)
```
Comments.Author: yeskunall

Comments.Body:
`./reset.css` path is relative to `styles.css` so Lume loads the file `/reset.css` instead of `/_includes/reset.css`.
Removing the relative path (simply `reset.css`) makes Lume to find the file in the `_includes` folder.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2974632657.Title: `@plugin "daisyui"` fails on latest tailwindcss plugin
2974632657.Author: kuboon
2974632657.Body:
### Version

v3 latest

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

```style.css
@import "tailwindcss";
@plugin "daisyui";
```

### What do you see instead?

```
TypeError: Attempted to load JSON module without specifying "type": "json" attribute in the import statement.
              const mod = await import(`npm:${id}`);
```

### Additional information

This simply fails because daisyui imports json in their `index.js`
```
> const mod = await import(`npm:daisyui`);
Uncaught TypeError: Attempted to load JSON module without specifying "type": "json" attribute in the import statement.
```

We need `with { type: 'json' }` for deno but they removed it.
https://github.com/saadeghi/daisyui/commit/24cd382bece0f072c220c51b966c56677d8ab10a

https://daisyui.com/docs/install/standalone/ is possible workaround, but currently `npm:` is hard-coded here 
https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/65a7ce4ec4572494987e9cd6758e0f160eeec2aa/plugins/tailwindcss.ts#L70

How do you think if plugin name starts with `./`, skip adding `npm:` ?
2974632657.PlainText:
@plugin "daisyui" fails on latest tailwindcss plugin

Version
v3 latest
What steps will reproduce the bug?
What do you see instead?
Additional information
This simply fails because daisyui imports json in their index.js
We need with { type: 'json' } for deno but they removed it.
 is possible workaround, but currently npm: is hard-coded here
How do you think if plugin name starts with ./, skip adding npm: ?

Comments.Body:
I use tailwind4 + daisyui5 with deno fresh with this unofficial plugin
https://github.com/pakornv/fresh-plugin-tailwindcss
They use postcss & `npm:@tailwindcss/postcss` to solve `@plugin`

It requires ` imports: {"daisyui": "npm:daisyui@^5.0.4",}` in deno.json but solves the problem straight forward.
Comments.Author: kuboon
oscarotero

2981190644.Title: HighlightJS plugin does not honor position with css placeholders
2981190644.Author: RickCogley
2981190644.PlainText:
HighlightJS plugin does not honor position with css placeholders

Version
f57483a1d5db94ba956525471b96155cda5810fa
Platform
macOS
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Setup highlightjs plugin, add placeholders to css in specific positions, see generated css, which just has the highlightjs css at the bottom of the file.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Consistently
What is the expected behavior?
I'd like to be able to specify the position of light and dark mode.
Given:
And in my styles.css:
If possible I would like the classes to be merged in position I specify, so they are picked up with the correct mode.
What do you see instead?
The css classes are merged in, at the bottom of the styles.css file:
The dark mode happened to be second, so it takes precedence, and even in light mode, the dark mode is displayed on the generated page.
Additional information
No response

Comments.Body:
I can't reproduce this error.
Perhaps you have another plugin to minify the CSS code before the codeHighlight plugin that removes the placeholders? If the placeholders are not found in the code, Lume insert the code at the end.

3010160079.Title: lume upgrade --dev fails to parse deno.json as jsonc
3010160079.Author: iacore
3010160079.Body:
### Version

/

### Platform

/

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Add an extra comma in `deno.json`, then run `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

100%

### What is the expected behavior?

It should work.

### What do you see instead?

```
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A - "upgrade" "--dev"
SyntaxError: Expected double-quoted property name in JSON at position 211 (line 9 column 3)
    at JSON.parse (<anonymous>)
    at https://deno.land/x/lume_init@v0.3.3/steps/load.ts:10:27
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:178:7)
    at async Init.run (https://deno.land/x/lume_init@v0.3.3/init.ts:115:22)
    at async default (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.3/cli/upgrade.ts:5:3)
    at async Command.fn (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.3/cli.ts:18:5)
    at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1794:7)
    at async Command.parseCommand (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1639:14)
    at async https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.3/cli.ts:175:3
```

### Additional information

I believe Deno uses json5 for its config, not `JSON.parse`.

Maybe elsewhere in Lume has this bug too.
3010160079.PlainText:
lume upgrade --dev fails to parse deno.json as jsonc

Version
/
Platform
/
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Add an extra comma in deno.json, then run deno task lume upgrade --dev.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
100%
What is the expected behavior?
It should work.
What do you see instead?
Additional information
I believe Deno uses json5 for its config, not JSON.parse.
Maybe elsewhere in Lume has this bug too.

Comments.Body:
Deno allows jsonc (json with comments), but not json5 (that is a different format).

Lume can read `deno.json` and `deno.jsonc`. I guess you're using jsonc format with the json extension, right?
The load function only use jsonc if the file extension is `.jsonc` ([source](https://github.com/lumeland/init/blob/main/steps/load.ts#L17)) Maybe it should be changed to use it for all formats.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> Maybe it should be changed to use it for all formats.

I see this behavior implemented now.
Comments.Author: iacore

### Version

### Platform

deno 2.2.7 (stable, release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu), should also reproduce on other platforms

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Create the following files in a newly set up Lume site, then run `lume`.

`a.md`:

```md
---
title: A
---

Page A
```

`b.md`:

```md
---
---

Page B
```

`index.vto`:

```md
---
title: Z
---

{{ for post of search.pages("", "title") }}
{{ post.basename }}
{{ /for }}
```

We request the pages to be sorted by `title`, but Page B lacks a `title` field. The comparator function ([created in `buildSort` in core/searcher.ts](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/v2.5.3/core/searcher.ts#L418)) tries to compare `undefined` with other strings. `undefined` gets converted to `NaN` and compared numerically with other strings, returning false on all comparisons. This violates the properties of comparator functions and causes undefined behavior in `Array#sort`.

The bug also affects the nav plugin, where directories without an index page are represented as `{ basename }` objects, lacking every field other than `basename`. It is therefore not currently possible to sort a nav menu properly by a custom field if pages skip levels.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

The bug is deterministically reproduced, but the precise result may depend on a lot of factors, including V8 version and filesystem state. Try changing the filenames and title fields, adding more files, etc., if the above example does not reproduce the bug.

### What is the expected behavior?

Possibilities:

- Throw an error if unsortable values exist.
- Force `undefined` to be sorted first or last.

### What do you see instead?

Pages are not sorted by `title`. In my case they are ordered as `index b a`.

### Additional information

_No response_

Comments.Body:
Thank you!
I'm working on the release of the new major version Lume 3 and will include a patch for this. Maybe a patch for Lume 2 can be released but I'm not sure yet.

As a temporary workaround, you can create a preprocessor to create the title variable in those pages that don't have it. For example:

```js
site.preprocess([".html"], (pages) => pages.forEach((page) => page.title ??= ""));
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I just made a change to ensure the field always exist using an empty string if the value is missing( `data[key] ?? ""`)

The fix will be published in Lume 3. If you want to try it, just run `deno task lume upgrade --dev`). Note that if your site is built with Lume 2, you will need to make some additional changes
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thanks for the quick fix! I can confirm that the latest commit works.

3032669010.Title: The path to backlinks
3032669010.Author: boehs
3032669010.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Hi, I'm futzing with Lume for my [site](https://github.com/boehs/site), which already uses Vento and Eleventy. Reading the docs I had some philosophical reservations, but I suspect many of them will be alleviated once I actually get to using Lume as its a bit of a paradigm shift in many regards (and, to be clear, it already looks like there's a lot of decisions I think are huge improvements)

The one thing I can't conceivably envision a workaround for is the backlinks feature, which displays on pages the other pages that link to that page (holy word salad). In Eleventy, I do this with a disgusting computedData hack. As far as I can tell, Lume doesn't have computed data which was a big shocker, but quite honestly maybe is an improvement.

At its most basic, the backlink feature requires the following things

1. The ability to export data generated during markdown template rendering

If I wanted I could implement my own loader to do this. This would be a mild hack but tolerable. It is *possible* that, as objects are reference types, I could modify the value of `data`?

```ts
  async function render(
    content: string,
    data?: Record<string, unknown>,
    filename?: string,
  )
```

is this officially supported? can it be? What would happen is my wikilinks plugin would, for each wikilink, do `data.linksTo.push('/path/to/page')`

2. Use this data in a layout

The layout would do something like `search.pages("linksTo.includes('{{thisPageUrl}}')")` (yes this syntax is invalid but you get the idea).

A different page could specify `renderOrder` so that all data is available. This is not possible here because backlinks are displayed in the page itself, so the *layout* would need to set renderOrder. According to order of operations, "For each group of pages with the same renderOrder .... Render the page using the assigned [template engine](https://lume.land/docs/core/multiple-template-engines/) and [layout](https://lume.land/docs/creating-pages/layouts/)," so this is not possible.

A really easy path to this that occurs to me would be to internally model layouts as pages. How this would work is when lume encounters the `layout` specifier, the page queue is amended as if the layout was a page itself, and is just passed `{{ content, ...data }}` for what the value of data would be. That way, layouts would by *default* be rendered fairly close to directly after the content page, but if `renderOrder` was set higher this would be deferred for free.

---

If you see any other options for how backlinks could be done today, or other paths to lume supporting this, please let me know! I'd be happy to discuss.

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Update: [I just made a change](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/commit/2602c19f6683a9eb257288253621cc58147867a9) to render the layouts once all pages of all renderOrder are rendered and looks like it works fine! (I could even detect a bug in a plugin).

This allows to use a markdown-it plugin to collect all backlinks pages, no matter the renderOrder.

I want to test this with real projects to make sure it doesn't break anything, but it seems a good decision. If you want to test it, just upgrade Lume to the latest development version with `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.

3035078749.Title: Transform Images plugin: Animation Lost When Input/Output Formats Are Not Both GIF
3035078749.Author: akimon658
3035078749.Body:
### Version

v2.5.2

### Platform

_No response_

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Process an animated image (GIF or WebP) with input and output formats that are not both GIF. Animation is lost during this processing.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Always, when processing an animated GIF or WebP image and the input and output formats are not both GIF.

### What is the expected behavior?

Animated images should remain animated after processing, regardless of the output format (unless a static output is explicitly intended).

### What do you see instead?

The output image is a static GIF or WebP, losing its animation.

### Additional information

Commit [62c5e0c](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/commit/62c5e0cdf6d61235805fb21979c1c83cb00f9a45) seems to have introduced a regression, limiting animation preservation to only GIF-to-GIF processing. This causes animated GIFs and WebP images to become static in all other conversion scenarios.
3035078749.PlainText:
Transform Images plugin: Animation Lost When Input/Output Formats Are Not Both GIF

Version
v2.5.2
Platform
No response
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Process an animated image (GIF or WebP) with input and output formats that are not both GIF. Animation is lost during this processing.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Always, when processing an animated GIF or WebP image and the input and output formats are not both GIF.
What is the expected behavior?
Animated images should remain animated after processing, regardless of the output format (unless a static output is explicitly intended).
What do you see instead?
The output image is a static GIF or WebP, losing its animation.
Additional information
Commit 62c5e0c seems to have introduced a regression, limiting animation preservation to only GIF-to-GIF processing. This causes animated GIFs and WebP images to become static in all other conversion scenarios.

Comments.Body:
The commit you mentioned fixed another issue: if an animated gif image is loaded and converted to a not animated format like jpeg, it converts all frames, creating a huge image with all frames that can cause the error `VipsJpeg: Maximum supported image dimension is 65500 pixels` if the image is too big.

(The error was reported in [a Discord conversation](https://discord.com/channels/794537085641818124/794537086278828094/1324884811696832727))

This is why only load all frames if the intention is maintain the animation. What other formats should support animations?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Given that Sharp's animation support is likely limited to GIF and WebP, focusing on preserving animation for these two formats would be sufficient to address the current issue.
Comments.Author: akimon658

Comments.Body:
Okay. I just made a change to preserve the animation between gif and webp formats.
It will be available in the next version
Comments.Author: oscarotero

3056532559.Title: Lume 3 StaticFile type change breaks support for cache_busting experimental plugin
3056532559.Author: marcus-crane
3056532559.Body:
### Version

3.0.1

### Platform

Linux + macOS

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Upon upgrading my site to Lume 3.0.1, with the experimental [cache_busting](https://github.com/lumeland/experimental-plugins/blob/main/cache_busting/mod.ts) plugin enabled, performing a build with `deno task lume` eventually results in the following error:

```console
error: Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: Cannot set property outputPath of #<StaticFile> which has only a getter
        staticFile.outputPath = `${path}.${hash}${ext}`;
                             ^
    at renameFile (file:///home/runner/work/utf9k/utf9k/_plugins/cache_busting.ts:111:30)
    at getHash (file:///home/runner/work/utf9k/utf9k/_plugins/cache_busting.ts:79:7)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:178:7)
    at async addHash (file:///home/runner/work/utf9k/utf9k/_plugins/cache_busting.ts:67:20)
    at async Object.replace [as fn] (file:///home/runner/work/utf9k/utf9k/_plugins/cache_busting.ts:47:18)
    at async https://deno.land/x/lume@v3.0.1/plugins/modify_urls.ts:81:15
    at async concurrent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v3.0.1/core/utils/concurrent.ts:17:7)
    at async Processors.run (https://deno.land/x/lume@v3.0.1/core/processors.ts:51:7)
    at async Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v3.0.1/core/site.ts:807:5)
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v3.0.1/core/site.ts:648:9)
Error: Process completed with exit code 1.
```

This makes sense as it seems that `outputPath` was converted from an attribute to a getter method with [this commit](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/commit/b676d3487ad1ea35e08e4b0c844bd79ce469564b)

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Consistently

### What is the expected behavior?

Ideally, `cache_busting` would be updated to support Lume v3 (or turned into a core plugin as it's pretty core to most SSGs I would think 😉) or the type signature for Lume v3 is reverted/altered

### What do you see instead?

As above

### Additional information

Personally, I had vendored the plugin to customise the caching format (to use dots instead of dashes) but it shouldn't have any impact as aside from that change, my copy is in sync with the latest experimental version.

This makes sense as it seems that outputPath was converted from an attribute to a getter method with this commit
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Comments.Body:
I just updated the plugin to work with Lume 3.
I'm agree that it's useful and should be included in the core. I'll work on that.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Ah great! All patched up 🙂 

I forgot that I had forked the cache_buster plugin and patched it so that it would just automatically hash any URI that was part of a `script` or `link` tag rather than using the `hash` attribute which is how the current experimental version works. The former is, as far as I'm aware, how most SSGs treat cache busting and seems like a nicer UX, at least in my opinion but there could be side effects with an implicit method that I'm not aware of (and have never seemingly run into personally)
Comments.Author: marcus-crane

Comments.Body: I can replace the `attribute` option with `selector`, so it can be `script,link` (to select all elements, like you approach) or `script[hash], link[hash]` (to select elements with the attribute `hash`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Sounds great! I'll mark this issue as closed anyway as I managed to apply your changes to my forked version and it all worked 🥳

3074605069.Title: Export slugifier for use by plugins
3074605069.Author: gordonbrander
3074605069.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Plugins may need access to the same sluggification logic used by core, so that they can generate URLs that match the rendered output. However, `createSlugifier` is not currently exported as part of the public API.

Suggestion: export `createSluggifier` from `mod.ts`, or perhaps from the sluggify plugin.

Plugins may need access to the same sluggification logic used by core, so that they can generate URLs that match the rendered output. However, createSlugifier is not currently exported as part of the public API.
Suggestion: export createSluggifier from mod.ts, or perhaps from the sluggify plugin.

3106810621.Title: Feed plugin does not seem to honor lang
3106810621.Author: RickCogley
3106810621.Body:
### Version

3.0.2

### Platform

MacOS

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

I have a multilanguage site at https://github.com/eSolia/blog.esolia.pro, published to https://blog.esolia.pro, and the settings I have made for the feed plugin are not taking effect. 

I have it set up here:
https://github.com/eSolia/blog.esolia.pro/blob/main/_config.ts#L336

The two feeds are being produced and the query filtering by lang is working. 

https://blog.esolia.pro/feed.en.json
https://blog.esolia.pro/feed.ja.json

If you look at both of these, 

* the description is Japanese in both although I have separate sections for both languages in my _data.yml:
   * https://github.com/eSolia/blog.esolia.pro/blob/main/src/_data.yml
* although I've specified the lang in the feed "info" and "items" both, it does not show up in the feeds
* the author in "info" doesn't show at all

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Consistently. I tried various combinations in the settings, but it's like it is not honoring or picking up the values in my _data.yml. 

### What is the expected behavior?

It should pick up and show fixed strings, and also be able to figure out the language sections of the _data.yml and display those correctly. 

### What do you see instead?

As mentioned, missing values.

### Additional information

this feels a bit like some of the other issues I had before, related to field aliases...
3106810621.PlainText:
Feed plugin does not seem to honor lang

Version
3.0.2
Platform
MacOS
What steps will reproduce the bug?
I have a multilanguage site at , published to , and the settings I have made for the feed plugin are not taking effect.
I have it set up here:
The two feeds are being produced and the query filtering by lang is working.
If you look at both of these,
the description is Japanese in both although I have separate sections for both languages in my _data.yml:
although I've specified the lang in the feed "info" and "items" both, it does not show up in the feeds
the author in "info" doesn't show at all
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Consistently. I tried various combinations in the settings, but it's like it is not honoring or picking up the values in my _data.yml.
What is the expected behavior?
It should pick up and show fixed strings, and also be able to figure out the language sections of the _data.yml and display those correctly.
What do you see instead?
As mentioned, missing values.
Additional information
this feels a bit like some of the other issues I had before, related to field aliases...

Comments.Body:
The variables for the feed info are taken from root global data. The multilanguage plugin only transform the data of the pages, not general data. The english data is stored in the `en` global property, so you can do this:

```js
site.use(feed({
  output: ["/feed.en.xml", "/feed.en.json"],
  query: "type=post lang=en",
  sort: "date=desc",
  info: {
    title: "=en.site.title",
    description: "=en.site.description",
    published: new Date(),
    lang: "en",
    generator: true,
    authorName: "eSolia Inc.",
  },
  // etc...
```

> although I've specified the lang in the feed "info" and "items" both, it does not show up in the feeds

`lang` is not used in the [JSON feed spec](https://www.jsonfeed.org/version/1/). But there's the `<language>` tag in the xml to set the language of the channel. There isn't a language tag per item, I don't know if there's a standard tag for that. I can add it if it's supported.

> the author in "info" doesn't show at all

I see the author in both xml and json formats.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
thanks for checking and explaining, @oscarotero. Ok, well noted on the lang being present only in the xml. I just wrongly assumed about the lang being in the items. 

Ok, after some further tests, what I found was, since the variables for "info" are taken from root global data as you mention, you need to have the language prefix on only the "sub" languages, not the default one. In other words, if it has a lang prefix in your _data.yml, then add it in the feed plugin config.

My second finding was, when I made these feed changes in my `_config.ts`, my `lume serve` wouldn't pick up the changes. Looking at the feeds on localhost:3000 showed no changes after the `lume serve` process said it was "reloading site" finally completed its load process, even if I force-refreshed the browser once it had restarted. 

It worked only after I did `rm -rf _site _cache deno.lock`. I'm not sure which of those had the impact, but once I did that, I could see the changes I made in the feed, from localhost:3000.

This is the config that worked, with only the secondary language of English having the prefix like `=en.site.title`: 

```ts
site.use(feed({
  output: ["/feed.ja.xml", "/feed.ja.json"],
  query: "type=post lang=ja",
  sort: "date=desc",
  info: {
    title: "=site.title",
    description: "=site.description",
    published: new Date(),
    lang: "ja",
    hubs: undefined,
    generator: true,
    authorName: "=site.author",
  },
  items: {
    title: "=title", // title of the item
    description: "=description", // description 
    published: "=date", // publishing date 
    updated: "=last_modified", // last update 
    lang: "ja", // language 
    image: "=image", // image 
    authorName: "=author", // author 
  },
}));
site.use(feed({
  output: ["/feed.en.xml", "/feed.en.json"],
  query: "type=post lang=en",
  sort: "date=desc",
  info: {
    title: "=en.site.title",
    description: "=en.site.description",
    published: new Date(),
    lang: "en",
    hubs: undefined,
    generator: true,
    authorName: "=en.site.author",
  },
  items: {
    title: "=title", // title of the item
    description: "=description", // description 
    published: "=date", // publishing date 
    updated: "=last_modified", // last update 
    lang: "en", // language 
    image: "=image", // image 
    authorName: "=author", // author 
  },
}));
```
Comments.Author: RickCogley

Comments.Body:
When `_config.ts` is changed, Lume needs to be reloaded entirely. This is automatically done by the lume command hence that's the "reloading site" message you see in the terminal.

The hot reload feature of lume server only works inside HTML pages and can reload the html page itself or other resources loaded by the pages (css, js, images, etc). But a xml/json feed file cannot be reload automatically. Once the build is completed, if you reload manually the browser, you should see the changes.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thanks. I tried reloading the browser after the Lume serve settled but nothing changed. I'm using Edge so it might be that. I didn't try any others this time.
Comments.Author: RickCogley

3117441224.Title: gzip tests fail on deno 2.3.3
3117441224.Author: Garciat
3117441224.Body:
Commit: 8856dabc49951566e76926800b335f9d4bddd737 (`main` at the time of posting) Deno: 2.3.3 (latest on Homebrew) System: `macOS 15.5 24F74 arm64` Command: `deno task test tests/gzip.test.ts` Some findings: - The tests output indicates that there is a slight difference in the compressed payload size between the snapshot and my local run. - e.g. `Uint8Array(82)` (snapshot) vs `Uint8Array(78)` (mine) - Recent CI test runs for macos [are passing](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/actions/runs/15414818356/job/43375038370). - Although the OS version is 14.7.6. - It seems like `macos-latest` [refers](https://github.com/actions/runner-images/blob/d8c2fb464ab0965a7cc86255d8903f539e80f135/README.md?plain=1#L28) to `macos-14` and not `macos-15`. - ❇ **AI**: it would be good to include `macos-15` in the test matrix, as [14.x only has <25% market share as of 2025](https://telemetrydeck.com/survey/apple/macOS/versions/). - Deno's compression seems to be implemented [here](https://github.com/denoland/deno/tree/v2.3.3/ext/web). It [depends on](https://github.com/denoland/deno/blob/v2.3.3/ext/web/Cargo.toml#L24) the `flate2` library built with `default` features, which uses a Rust backend (`miniz_oxide`). - If that's true, the system's `zlib` should not be a factor here. (Something I considered based on https://github.com/mozilla/pdf.js/issues/17399) - And if that's true, the above macOS version discrepancy should also not be a factor. - The remaining discrepancy would be my Deno version. However, the version of `flate2` (1.0.30) hasn't changed between [2.3.3](https://github.com/denoland/deno/blame/v2.3.3/Cargo.toml#L157) and [2.3.5](https://github.com/denoland/deno/blame/v2.3.5/Cargo.toml#L160). - Still, I manually downloaded 2.3.5 and ran the tests with it. They passed. Conclusions: - There is nothing to do here. If other macOS users run into this issue, they should upgrade their Deno version. Unfortunately, Homebrew seems to be stuck on 2.3.3 per https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/pull/225214. (PS: I investigated this for fun 😄)

gzip tests fail on deno 2.3.3

System: macOS 15.5 24F74 arm64

The tests output indicates that there is a slight difference in the compressed payload size between the snapshot and my local run.
e.g. Uint8Array(82) (snapshot) vs Uint8Array(78) (mine)
Recent CI test runs for macos are passing.
Although the OS version is 14.7.6.
It seems like macos-latest refers to macos-14 and not macos-15.
❇  AI: it would be good to include macos-15 in the test matrix, as 14.x only has <25% market share as of 2025.
Deno's compression seems to be implemented here. It depends on the flate2 library built with default features, which uses a Rust backend (miniz_oxide).
If that's true, the system's zlib should not be a factor here. (Something I considered based on )
And if that's true, the above macOS version discrepancy should also not be a factor.
The remaining discrepancy would be my Deno version. However, the version of flate2 (1.0.30) hasn't changed between 2.3.3 and 2.3.5.
Still, I manually downloaded 2.3.5 and ran the tests with it. They passed.
Conclusions:
There is nothing to do here. If other macOS users run into this issue, they should upgrade their Deno version. Unfortunately, Homebrew seems to be stuck on 2.3.3 per .
(PS: I investigated this for fun 😄)

3126825660.Title: Sitemap plugin mangles already existing robots.txt file
3126825660.Author: tudurom
3126825660.PlainText:
Sitemap plugin mangles already existing robots.txt file

Version
v3.0.2
Platform
Linux amd64
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Have a non-empty robots.txt file in your site's source directory.
My robots.txt: 
Use the sitemap plugin in your config.
Go to http://localhost:3000/robots.txt.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Every time I build the website.
What is the expected behavior?
I would expect the generated robots.txt file to be the same as the one I have in my repository, plus the Sitemap directive at the end.
What do you see instead?
The original contents seem to be replaced with a sequence of numbers that are probably the ASCII representation of each character in my original robots.txt file.
Generated robots.txt:
Additional information
No response

Comments.Body:
Yeah, this is a bug. Thanks for reporting it.
I just fixed and it will be available in the next Lume version.
Meanwhile, you can upgrade Lume to the latest development version with `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

3186154219.Title: Access to All Environment Variables Is Required
3186154219.Author: foster-hangdaan
3186154219.Body:
### Version

3.0.4

### Platform

Linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Running the development server using the following command:

```sh
deno run --allow-write='.' --allow-read='.' --allow-net='deno.land,cdn.deno.land,esm.sh,0.0.0.0:3000,localhost:3000,jsr.io,lumeland.github.io,code.hangdaan.com' --allow-import=code.hangdaan.com,deno.land,jsr.io,cdn.jsdelivr.net --allow-sys=networkInterfaces --allow-env=JSR_URL lume.ts -s
```

As well as building the site:

```sh
deno run --allow-write='.' --allow-read='.' --allow-net='deno.land,cdn.deno.land,esm.sh,0.0.0.0:3000,localhost:3000,jsr.io,lumeland.github.io,code.hangdaan.com' --allow-import=code.hangdaan.com,deno.land,jsr.io,cdn.jsdelivr.net --allow-sys=networkInterfaces --allow-env=JSR_URL lume.ts
```

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Everytime.

### What is the expected behavior?

To take advantage of Deno's security features, Lume should require the minimum amount of permissions. Building the site, and running the development server, should work **without** granting permission to all environment variables with the `--allow-env` flag.

### What do you see instead?

Lume will ask for permission to access all environment variables:

```
┏  Deno requests env access.
┠─ Requested by `Deno.permissions.request()` API.
┠─ To see a stack trace for this prompt, set the DENO_TRACE_PERMISSIONS environmental variable.
┠─ Learn more at: https://docs.deno.com/go/--allow-env
┠─ Run again with --allow-env to bypass this prompt.
┗ Allow? [y/n/A] (y = yes, allow; n = no, deny; A = allow all env permissions) >
```

Denying the access request results in an error:

```
x Denied env access.
Loading config file file:///home/user/projects/website/_config.ts
error: Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: Module not found "https://deno.land/x/lume@v3.0.4/jsx-runtime".
    at https://deno.land/x/lume@v3.0.4/deps/ssx.ts:1:33
    const mod = await import(url);
                ^
    at async createSite (https://deno.land/x/lume@v3.0.4/cli/utils.ts:26:17)
    at async buildSite (https://deno.land/x/lume@v3.0.4/cli/utils.ts:45:16)
```

### Additional information

- Deno Version: 2.2.13
- I'm updating from Lume 2.5.3 which did not require access to all environment variables.
- This issue can be reproduced using [my repo](https://code.hangdaan.com/foster/website/src/commit/a6d4271d898dcef0f324e2f2cb9489917fccdf9f).
- This seems to be a regression of #551.
3186154219.PlainText:
Access to All Environment Variables Is Required

Version
3.0.4
Platform
Linux
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Running the development server using the following command:
As well as building the site:
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Everytime.
What is the expected behavior?
To take advantage of Deno's security features, Lume should require the minimum amount of permissions. Building the site, and running the development server, should work without granting permission to all environment variables with the --allow-env flag.
What do you see instead?
Lume will ask for permission to access all environment variables:
Denying the access request results in an error:
Additional information
Deno Version: 2.2.13
I'm updating from Lume 2.5.3 which did not require access to all environment variables.
This issue can be reproduced using my repo.
This seems to be a regression of #551.

Comments.Body:
Okay, that's fair.

The `env` Lume version sent a request to access to the environment variables [here.](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/00eb7e28f9bd0475a4e9ea62f31ffaedac8f85c5/core/utils/env.ts#L21)

I just removed this and now it only checks if it has access but doesn't request access ([new changes here](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/main/core/utils/env.ts#L36)).

If you want to test it, upgrade Lume to the latest development version editing the lume import to this:

```json
"lume/": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/lumeland/lume@9b09a8e2089f75ae9949ad541e65ba0147a72959/",
```

The error when the permissions are denied is not related with that. It's caused by the JSX configuration that has changed in Lume 3 (it no longer use React or Preact, [more info](https://lume.land/blog/posts/lume-3/#one-jsx-plugin)).

To fix it, add the following import in your deno.json file:

```json
"lume/jsx-runtime": "https://deno.land/x/ssx@v0.1.10/jsx-runtime.ts",
```
and configure the compiler to use `lume` as the JSX import source:


```json
"compilerOptions": {
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "jsxImportSource": "lume",
    "types": [
      "lume/types.ts"
    ]
  },
```

Other changed needed in Lume 3:
- Rename `index.tsx` to `index.page.tsx` (Lume 3 requires the `.page` subextension in jsx pages)
- Add `site.add([".scss"])` to the config.ts file (Lume 3 requires to [add the assets](https://lume.land/blog/posts/lume-3/#plugins-no-longer-load-files-automatically) explicitly)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:

> 
> I just removed this and now it only checks if it has access but doesn't request access ([new changes here](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/main/core/utils/env.ts#L36)).
>

> "lume/": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/lumeland/lume@9b09a8e2089f75ae9949ad541e65ba0147a72959/",

I have tested on my end and can confirm that latest dev version above has fixed the permission issue.

>

3251482945.Title: JSX: `precompile` for `compilerOptions.jsx` is broken
3251482945.Author: satgo1546
3251482945.Body:
### Version 3.0.5 ### Platform Arch Linux (WSL 2); should be reproducible on other OSes ### What steps will reproduce the bug? 1. `deno run -A https://lume.land/init.ts` to initialize a new site. 2. Choose **Basic + plugins**, then select **jsx**. 3. Edit `deno.json` to use `precompile` for `compilerOptions.jsx`. ```diff "compilerOptions": { "types": [ "lume/types.ts" ], - "jsx": "react-jsx", + "jsx": "precompile", "jsxImportSource": "lume" }, ``` 4. Create a test page (`index.page.tsx`, for example). ```tsx export default () =>

test page

``` 5. `deno task lume` and inspect the output. ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Always reproducible in Lume 3.0.5. ### What is the expected behavior? An HTML page with a heading. This is observed in Lume 3.0.4 and older. ```html

test page

``` ### What do you see instead? The `

` tags are escaped, so the browser renders the tags as text. ```html <h1>test page</h1> ``` ### Additional information Lume docs do not mention whether precompile is supported. #699 suggests it was supported for `jsx-preact` but not `jsx` (React) in Lume 2.x. The `ssx` package readme mentions that it's "compatible with the `precompile` option available in Deno", so I assume `precompile` is supported in Lume 3 as well.
3251482945.PlainText:
JSX: precompile for compilerOptions.jsx is broken Version 3.0.5 Platform Arch Linux (WSL 2); should be reproducible on other OSes What steps will reproduce the bug? deno run -A to initialize a new site. Choose Basic + plugins, then select jsx. Edit deno.json to use precompile for compilerOptions.jsx. Create a test page (index.page.tsx, for example). deno task lume and inspect the output. How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Always reproducible in Lume 3.0.5. What is the expected behavior? An HTML page with a heading. This is observed in Lume 3.0.4 and older. What do you see instead? The

tags are escaped, so the browser renders the tags as text. Additional information Lume docs do not mention whether precompile is supported. #699 suggests it was supported for jsx-preact but not jsx (React) in Lume 2.x. The ssx package readme mentions that it's "compatible with the precompile option available in Deno", so I assume precompile is supported in Lume 3 as well.

Comments.Body:
Precompile mode is not used in Lume because it's incompatible with other plugins that use JSX under the hood, like MDX or OG Images.

But I agree that, if defined, I should work with `.page.tsx` files. I'll take a look.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I just fixed this, and will be available in the next Lume version (3.0.6).
You can use it upgrading your Lume version to the latest dev version `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero
