1795380343.Title: Slugification for copied files
1795380343.Author: svlauer
1795380343.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Thank you for creating lume, I love it so far!

One thing that would be great would be a way to slugify also the names of `copy()`ed files. Because right now, the interaction of copying and slugification seems kind of bad.

Unless I am missing something (which might well be!), right now, the behavior is the following (assuming the slugify plugin is loaded):

* Suppose in my `.src` directory contains a path `with spaces and Capitals/`, which contains:
    *  a static file `with spaces and Capitals/more spaces and Capitals.bin` (with `site.copy(['.bin']`)
    *  a processed file  `with spaces and Capitals/With Spaces.md`
* I'll be getting two directories in `_site`:
    *  `with spaces and Capitals`, which contains `more spaces and Capitals.bin`
    *  `with-spaces-and-capitals`, which contains `with-spaces.html`
* Ideally, I would like to have:
    * One directory,  `with-spaces-and-capitals`, which contains `more-spaces-and-capitals.bin` and  `with-spaces.html`
* I know I can fiddle with the url of the `copy()`ed files via the second argument to `copy()`, but such a solution would be cumbersome and somewhat brittle (e.g., suppose the slugify options change).

I guess one of the reasons for the current behavior is that `copy()`ed files are not processed at all, hence the slugify plugin has no chance to intervene.

Maybe it would be an option to provide (in the slugify plugin?) a `copyAndSlugify()` method?

Or is my "ideally" above already possible, I just missed how?

If not, I'd be willing to work on a pull request for this.
1795380343.PlainText:
Slugification for copied files

Enter your suggestions in details:
Thank you for creating lume, I love it so far!
One thing that would be great would be a way to slugify also the names of copy()ed files. Because right now, the interaction of copying and slugification seems kind of bad.
Unless I am missing something (which might well be!), right now, the behavior is the following (assuming the slugify plugin is loaded):
Suppose in my .src directory contains a path with spaces and Capitals/, which contains:
a static file with spaces and Capitals/more spaces and Capitals.bin (with site.copy(['.bin'])
a processed file  with spaces and Capitals/With Spaces.md
I'll be getting two directories in _site:
with spaces and Capitals, which contains more spaces and Capitals.bin
with-spaces-and-capitals, which contains with-spaces.html
Ideally, I would like to have:
One directory,  with-spaces-and-capitals, which contains more-spaces-and-capitals.bin and  with-spaces.html
I know I can fiddle with the url of the copy()ed files via the second argument to copy(), but such a solution would be cumbersome and somewhat brittle (e.g., suppose the slugify options change).
I guess one of the reasons for the current behavior is that copy()ed files are not processed at all, hence the slugify plugin has no chance to intervene.
Maybe it would be an option to provide (in the slugify plugin?) a copyAndSlugify() method?
Or is my "ideally" above already possible, I just missed how?
If not, I'd be willing to work on a pull request for this.

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Thanks for the quick reply!

> ```js
> site.use(slugifyUrls({
>   staticFiles: true,
> });
> ```

Seems sensible - in principle, I think it would even make sense to have this option activated by default ... but that would be a breaking change. 

Thank you for the workaround, that makes things easier for me in the meantime!
Comments.Author: svlauer
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Works like a charm! 

Thank you for making this happen so quickly!

A suggestion/question: Wouldn't it make sense to change the default to `extensions: "*"` for lume 2? Like, if someone wants slugification, they probably want it for all/most things?
(I might be missing something that makes this a bad idea, and anyways, having an extra line for this in _config.ts is not a big deal, just wondering.)
Comments.Author: svlauer

Comments.Body:
Yes, good idea. I'll change it to `*` in v2.
Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

1824778292.Title: Add JSON-LD plugin
1824778292.Author: oscarotero
1824778292.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

More info: https://jsonld.com/
It could use: https://www.npmjs.com/package/schema-dts
Inspo: https://github.com/codiume/orbit/tree/main/packages/astro-seo-schema
1824778292.PlainText:
Add JSON-LD plugin

Enter your suggestions in details:
More info: 
It could use: 
Inspo:

Comments.Body: Hi, can I do this? I think the best way is to modify the metas plugin, what do you think?
Comments.Author: shuaixr

Comments.Body:
Hey @shuaixr 
Sure, thanks for your help!!

I don't think we should include this in the metas plugin, because JSON-LD is too big and complex.
Take a look to [astro-seo-schema](https://www.npmjs.com/package/astro-seo-schema) and [astro-seo-meta](https://www.npmjs.com/package/astro-seo-meta), they are different packages and I think Lume plugins should separate them too.

JSON-LD is hard, so I'd like a plugin that help to create entities easily. Maybe using a library like https://www.npmjs.com/package/schema-dts could be nice because it provides types to autocomplete the values. But would like to investigate if we can do it even easier. Let's see this example:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': 'https://my.site/#alyssa',
  name: 'Alyssa P. Hacker',
  hasOccupation: {
    '@type': 'Occupation',
    name: 'LISP Hacker',
    qualifications: 'Knows LISP',
  },
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
},
```

It should handle the URLs of the pages. The URLs `https://my.site/#alyssa` and `https://my.site/about/#page` should be managed by Lume, so if the site is built with a different url (`deno task build --location https://other.site`) all these URLs should change. I imagine a script that search recursively by all `@id` and `url` keys that doesn't contain the domain and complete them. For example:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': '#alyssa',
  name: 'Alyssa P. Hacker',
  hasOccupation: {
    '@type': 'Occupation',
    name: 'LISP Hacker',
    qualifications: 'Knows LISP',
  },
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': '/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': '/about/#page'},
},
```

It must allow aliases, in the same way as metas. For example:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': '#alyssa',
  name: '=user.name',
  hasOccupation: {
    '@type': 'Occupation',
    name: '=user.occupation.name',
    qualifications: '=user.occupation.qualifications',
  },
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': '/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': '/about/#page'},
},
```

It should be able to show or hide entities if some values are empty. For example, if the user doesn't have occupation, instead of output the properties with empty values:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': 'https://my.site/#alyssa',
  name: 'Alyssa P. Hacker',
  hasOccupation: {
    '@type': 'Occupation',
    name: '',
    qualifications: '',
  },
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
},
```

It should omit this object:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': 'https://my.site/#alyssa',
  name: 'Alyssa P. Hacker',
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
},
```

It would be nice if the plugin checked the data structure, in order to detect missing properties, or not found relations between entities.

What do you think? I know it's complex, so we can start with the basics and improve it little by little.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: After closer look at the metas plugin, I think you're right. I'll open a draft pr soon
Comments.Author: shuaixr

1831432664.Title: Allow to customize the data merging from _config

For example `site.mergeKey("keyname", "merge mode")`:

site.mergeKey("site", "object");

site.mergeKey("site", (parent, current) => ({ ...parent, ...current}));

And even define scope (for example, only in `/posts` folder:

site.mergeKey("site", "object", "/posts");
```

1833550738.Title: copying "Hello World" example renders garbled text
1833550738.Author: stevietrouble
1833550738.Body:
### Version 1.18.3 ### Platform Windows ### What steps will reproduce the bug? ``` deno run -Ar https://deno.land/x/lume/init.ts > _config.ts > Maybe Later // Important: Copy-pasted from terminal echo "hello world" > index.md deno task serve ``` ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Every time I try it. ### What is the expected behavior? ```

Hello world

``` ### What do you see instead? ```

��H�e�l�l�o� �w�o�r�l�d� � �

``` ### Additional information Running latest Deno built from source with `cargo install deno --locked` **The issue is that Windows Powershell's echo command outputs text in UTF-16, and Lume reads it as UTF-8.**
1833550738.PlainText:
copying "Hello World" example renders garbled text

Version
1.18.3
Platform
Windows
What steps will reproduce the bug?
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Every time I try it.
What is the expected behavior?
What do you see instead?
Additional information
Running latest Deno built from source with cargo install deno --locked
The issue is that Windows Powershell's echo command outputs text in UTF-16, and Lume reads it as UTF-8.

Comments.Body:
Thanks for reporting this bug, but I'm not sure if this can be fixed easily. Lume uses [`Deno.readTextFile`](https://deno.land/api@v1.35.3?s=Deno.readTextFile) function to read the text content and, indeed the function description makes it clear that the file is read as UTF-8 decoded string.

I guess this is a very edge case, but l'm leaving this open just in case someone can provide quick and easy solution for that.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: One easy fix could be suggesting the user "create an index.md file" rather than suggesting using echo. Or, having the template start off with the hello world file already added.
Comments.Author: stevietrouble

Comments.Body:
Okay, I just changed the quick start tutorial with your suggestion. It will be available in the next version.
thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
To add to the list of options, I've created a [Kroki plugin for Lume](https://code.hangdaan.com/foster/lume-plugin-kroki) which converts codeblocks containing diagram descriptions into an `<img/>` during the site build. It currently has support for all of the [Kroki Diagram Types](https://kroki.io/#support); Mermaid being one of them.

The images are rendered by a [Kroki](https://kroki.io/) server. By default, the plugin uses the official instance at https://kroki.io, but can be configured to use a self-hosted instance.

1856972803.Title: Share optimized image into several locations if size and format are identical
1856972803.Author: cawa-93
1856972803.Body: ### Enter your suggestions in details: Let's say I have two components for showing same image: ```html ``` First one resolved into `my-pic-300w.png` and `my-pic-300w@2.png`. Second one into `my-pic-600w.png` and `my-pic-600w@2.png`. The problem is that files `my-pic-300w@2.png` and `my-pic-600w.png` are completely identical but they generated twice, stored in cache twice, stores in fs twice and loaded into client twice. Maybe it have sence to resolve file size and share one file into several locations? So in my example instead of having 4 files we will get only 3: `my-pic-300w.png`, `my-pic-600w.png`, `my-pic-1200w.png`.

1865656526.Title: Import NPM modules using ranges
1865656526.Author: binyamin
1865656526.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

### Summary
When importing an NPM module, use a caret range (ie. `npm:preact@^10.16.0`). If a user has another compatible version of the module installed, Deno will just use that one instead of downloading another one. In other words, Deno's npm specifiers support semver ranges, so let's use them.

### Notes

Esbuild imports should probably use a tilde, so only patch updates will be affected, because [esbuild makes breaking changes in minor updates](https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0190).

#### Syntax
- caret "^" allows minor & patch updates
- tilde "~" only allows patch updates
1865656526.PlainText:
Import NPM modules using ranges

Enter your suggestions in details:
Summary
When importing an NPM module, use a caret range (ie. npm:preact@^10.16.0). If a user has another compatible version of the module installed, Deno will just use that one instead of downloading another one. In other words, Deno's npm specifiers support semver ranges, so let's use them.
Notes
Esbuild imports should probably use a tilde, so only patch updates will be affected, because esbuild makes breaking changes in minor updates.
Syntax
caret "^" allows minor & patch updates
tilde "~" only allows patch updates

Comments.Body: For now, I prefer to continue using fixed versions. It's much more stable and easy to debug. I know Deno is moving toward semver compatibility (as announced for Deno 2.0) but I'm not fully convinced about this change and rather to wait.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: @oscarotero I haven't read the news about Deno 2.0. I did some testing, and ever since Deno stabilized NPM specifiers in v1.28.0, it correctly resolves semver in NPM specifiers.
Comments.Author: binyamin

Comments.Body:
When I mention stability, I'm not referring to Deno but the packages in general. One of the good things of Lume is the simplicity. If you want to use postcss, just import the plugin and use it. You don't need to worry about which version is used or if you need to update it because a minor release just came out.

Using fixed versions allows to test Lume in a reliable way. Ranges can introduce new bugs or inconsitencies with the Lume plugin, even in patch updates. Every time a new version of Lume is released, all dependencies are updated and tested ([using udd](https://github.com/hayd/deno-udd)). In the edge case that you need a different version, it's possible to patch it with the import maps.

Maybe in the future I change my mind, but for now I'd rather to continue using fixed versions.
If you want to take a look what's coming to Deno 2.0, this video explains it well: https://youtu.be/5DX49vzLfqw?si=hkoKe3EWuHeez27K&t=820
Comments.Author: oscarotero

1867990439.Title: Layout doesn't applied to `.js` urls
1867990439.Author: cawa-93
1867990439.Body:
### Version

v1.18.4

### Platform

Windows 11

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Create a generator for `.js` file using layout

```js
// app.tmpl.js

const url = './app.js'
const layout = 'layout.njk'
```

```njk
<!-- _includes/layout.njk -->
console.log('app.js')
```
And run `serve`. 

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
The only problem is when you try to generate the `.js` file. If you generate the usual html (`export const url = './app/'`), the layout is applied as expected.


### What is the expected behavior?

single `app.js` file with `console.log('app.js')` should be generated

### What do you see instead?

```node
⚠️ Warning Skipped page /app.js (file content is empty)
```
Although obviously the file should not be empty,

### Additional information

_No response_
1867990439.PlainText:
Layout doesn't applied to .js urls

Version
v1.18.4
Platform
Windows 11
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Create a generator for .js file using layout
And run serve.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
The only problem is when you try to generate the .js file. If you generate the usual html (export const url = './app/'), the layout is applied as expected.
What is the expected behavior?
single app.js file with console.log('app.js') should be generated
What do you see instead?
Although obviously the file should not be empty,
Additional information
No response

Comments.Body:
Layouts are used only for `.html` pages.
Any page that generates a non-html page (js, css, jpg, etc) doesn't not use the layout. 
See [regular pages vs asset pages](https://lume.land/docs/core/concepts/#page-files).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

1868177875.Title: Support content blocks?
1868177875.Author: mlanza
1868177875.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Nunjucks has a feature called blocks.  It's useful when you have different sections of a certain page type.  Without blocks you can inline your markdown in the yaml.  

```yaml
credits: |
  * <label>Designers</label>: Michael Kiesling & Wolfgang Kramer
  * <label>Illustrators</label>: Paul Mafayon & Christophe Swal
```

Inlining in the yaml is fine with small bits of content but is awkward when you have several sections each with substantial content.  Blocks are better for this.

```md
{% block credits %}
* <label>Designers</label>: Michael Kiesling & Wolfgang Kramer
* <label>Illustrators</label>: Paul Mafayon & Christophe Swal
{% endblock %}
```

The blocks feature exists in Nunjucks but it didn't seem to work for me, so I wasn't sure if Lume as a wrapper implements it.  I didn't see any examples taking advantage of blocks, even in the other templating languages.  

I saw it rejected in #314 but I wasn't sure if it was the feature as a whole or a user-specific issue, since it seems relatively important when it comes to structured content writing.  Maybe one of the other templating languages supports the concept?  With the current design, I have pages with 3-4 sections in which I have to jam much of the prose into the front matter.  I was looking at it and it didn't seem right my content should live up in the yaml.
1868177875.PlainText:
Support content blocks?

Enter your suggestions in details:
Nunjucks has a feature called blocks.  It's useful when you have different sections of a certain page type.  Without blocks you can inline your markdown in the yaml.
Inlining in the yaml is fine with small bits of content but is awkward when you have several sections each with substantial content.  Blocks are better for this.
The blocks feature exists in Nunjucks but it didn't seem to work for me, so I wasn't sure if Lume as a wrapper implements it.  I didn't see any examples taking advantage of blocks, even in the other templating languages.
I saw it rejected in #314 but I wasn't sure if it was the feature as a whole or a user-specific issue, since it seems relatively important when it comes to structured content writing.  Maybe one of the other templating languages supports the concept?  With the current design, I have pages with 3-4 sections in which I have to jam much of the prose into the front matter.  I was looking at it and it didn't seem right my content should live up in the yaml.

Comments.Body:
Blocks cannot be used with the Lume layouts because it's not possible to pass the blocks defined by one template to other template. For example:

```njk
---
layout: main.njk
---

{% block credits %}
* <label>Designers</label>: Michael Kiesling & Wolfgang Kramer
* <label>Illustrators</label>: Paul Mafayon & Christophe Swal
{% endblock %}
```

Lume cannot get the `credits` block and pass to `main.njk` (at least I wasn't able to find a way to do that).
But you can use the nunjucks native layouts, that does that:

```njk
{% extends "main.njk" %}

{% block credits %}
* <label>Designers</label>: Michael Kiesling & Wolfgang Kramer
* <label>Illustrators</label>: Paul Mafayon & Christophe Swal
{% endblock %}
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thank you.
Comments.Author: mlanza

1868380774.Title: Add a `.serve()` function to the site value
1868380774.Author: leighmcculloch
1868380774.PlainText:
Add a .serve() function to the site value

Enter your suggestions in details:
Lume allows using it as a library only without the CLI. For my workflows I've found that much easier to reason about than using the CLI. For example, in my sites instead of a _config.ts, I have a script for building, build.ts and that script creates the site, attaches plugins, etc, and then finally calls site.build().
Then, for a serve variant, I have another file serve.ts that contains:
Given that lume provides the simple ability to build with site.build(), it would be convenient if the serve functionality of the CLI was moved from the CLI into the site value. So that it would be possible to simply call site.serve().
Related discussion at .

Comments.Body:
Lume local server uses the [`Server` class](https://lume.land/docs/core/server/) with some middlewares to handle 404 responses, prevent browser cache, watch the file changes and live reload.

Including all this logic inside the `Site` class would make the class more complicated, with too much responsabilities.

You can instantiate the Server class and add the [following middlewares](https://lume.land/docs/core/server/) to recreate the same server provided by Lume.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

1889941813.Title: Co-locating imported CSS files with the PostCSS plugin
1889941813.Author: cgwrench
1889941813.Body:
I'm getting started with Lume and trying to locate `@import`-ed CSS files in the same directory as the files that import them. I'm using the PostCSS plugin.

All the examples and documentation I've found so far place the imported files in a different directory, usually in a sub-directory of `_includes`. Is there a way to have CSS files and the files they import in the same directory?

For example, the [Lume base blog example](https://github.com/lumeland/base-blog) has a main CSS file, `styles.css`, in the root of the repository, which imports other CSS files from an `_includes/css` directory. These files are structured as follows:

```
├── _includes
│   └── css
│       ├── navbar.css
│       ├── post-list.css
│       └── …
└── styles.css
```

`styles.css` contains `@import` at-rules like:

```css
…
@import "css/reset.css";
@import "css/navbar.css";
…
```

I'd prefer to have files structured as follows:

```
└── css
    ├── navbar.css
    ├── post-list.css
    ├── …
    └── styles.css
```

And my `styles.css` would contain `@import` at-rules like:

```css
…
@import "reset.css";
@import "navbar.css";
…
```

In other words, the imports are resolved relative to the file containing the imports, not relative to the `_includes` directory.

I've managed to get this working with the following configuration:

```js
site
  .use(postcss({
    includes: "css/" // trailing '/' matters here
  }))
```

Note that the trailing `/` matters here - without it, no CSS files are created in the `_site` directory. This feels like a bit of a hack - is this the way to achieve the file structure I'm looking for, or is there a better way?

1894203445.Title: Page with `url: false` not retured by `search.pages`
1894203445.Author: hannobraun
1894203445.Body:
### Version

1.18.5

### Platform

Linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. Search for pages by tag in a JSX template (e.g. `search.pages("update")`).
2. Confirm that this works as expected.
3. Set `url: false` for those pages.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Reproduces every time.

### What is the expected behavior?

I expect the behavior of `search.pages` to not change, as [the documentation states](https://lume.land/docs/creating-pages/urls/#setting-url-to-false) that "\[the page\] is still visible by other pages, for example, in paginations"

### What do you see instead?

The same call to `search.pages` that previously worked as expected now returns an empty array.

### Additional information

I did some digging around the code. The `Searcher` [gets a reference to `this.pages` in the `Site` constructor](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/b4b12ce0c11ff64d52703566463575bf6e40f415/core/site.ts#L190), then `this.pages` is [later modified to remove pages with `url === false`](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/b4b12ce0c11ff64d52703566463575bf6e40f415/core/site.ts#L642-L648). This looks like it would also modify the array that the `Searcher` got. Maybe the `Searcher` needs a copy of that array, not a reference to it? (Sorry if this analysis is wrong. My JavaScript skills are very rusty, and my TypeScript skills very new.)
1894203445.PlainText:
Page with url: false not retured by search.pages

Version
1.18.5
Platform
Linux
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Search for pages by tag in a JSX template (e.g. search.pages("update")).
Confirm that this works as expected.
Set url: false for those pages.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Reproduces every time.
What is the expected behavior?
I expect the behavior of search.pages to not change, as the documentation states that "[the page] is still visible by other pages, for example, in paginations"
What do you see instead?
The same call to search.pages that previously worked as expected now returns an empty array.
Additional information
I did some digging around the code. The Searcher gets a reference to this.pages in the Site constructor, then this.pages is later modified to remove pages with url === false. This looks like it would also modify the array that the Searcher got. Maybe the Searcher needs a copy of that array, not a reference to it? (Sorry if this analysis is wrong. My JavaScript skills are very rusty, and my TypeScript skills very new.)

Comments.Body:
I think the issue is in the documentation. Probably this is the old behavior and at some point this was changed but the documentation wasn't updated.

Reimplement this behavior is complicated, because `search.pages()` only returns HTML pages, Lume consider a page is HTML if the url contains the `.html` extension (no matter if it's normal URL `/foo.html` or pretty URL `/foo/index.html`). But pages with url as `false` cannot be considered HTML pages, so they wouldn't be returned by `search.pages()` even if they weren't removed from the `site.pages` array.

Why do you need this? Maybe I can help you with a workaround.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Ah, that makes sense. Thank you for the explanation!

> Why do you need this? Maybe I can help you with a workaround.

I'm [building a page](https://www.hannobraun.com/) with article-like pages that I want to keep updated. For each update, I want to display some information (date of the update, link to the page that was updated, and a short description of the update) in several places:

- A "latest updates" list on the home page (already implemented)
- An RSS feed (currently working on it)
- A changelog on each updated page

Storing these updates as pages works well, as that gives me access via `search.pages` where I need it (except in the feed; might open another issue about that later :smile:) and allows me to store all relevant information as Markdown + front matter.

However, I don't actually want a page per update, hence `url: false`. My current workaround is to have a template for these updates that displays a 404-like page instead.

This works well enough, but it's not exactly pretty. And a "page" is the wrong abstraction to use anyway, since I don't actually want a page. I did it because I'm not aware of any better abstractions within Lume. But now that I think about it, I can just store those updates as regular files, load them using Deno APIs, and circumvent Lume completely. That's probably the better solution.
Comments.Author: hannobraun
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Oh, nice, thank you!

So many useful plugins. Hard to keep them all straight :smile:
Comments.Author: hannobraun

1894917537.Title: Simplify Twitter Card Tags and Remove Unused Keywords Meta Tag
1894917537.Author: ghost
1894917537.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

## Issue Description

The current implementation of the `metas.ts` plugin in Lume includes unnecessary Twitter Card tags (`twitter:*`) even when OpenGraph tags (`og:*`) are present. Twitter provides fallback behavior for OpenGraph tags, which means that we don't need to duplicate our efforts by including both sets of tags.

Additionally, the keywords meta tag is no longer utilized by search engines for SEO purposes. Therefore, it can be safely removed to reduce output sizes and maintain consistent development practices.

## Proposed Changes

- Remove the `twitter:title` tag if `og:title` is present, optimizing the Twitter Card tags.
- Simplify Twitter Card tags to include only `twitter:site` and `twitter:card` when `og:*` tags are present, eliminating redundant tags.
- Consider removing the keywords meta tag as it is no longer effective for SEO, resulting in a cleaner codebase and reduced output size.

## Additional Information

- [Twitter Card Markup Documentation](https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/twitter-for-websites/cards/overview/markup) provides details on Twitter's card markup requirements.
- [Getting Started with Twitter Cards](https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/twitter-for-websites/cards/guides/getting-started) offers guidance on implementing Twitter Cards effectively.
- [Social Media Meta Tags](https://head-2ml.pages.dev/#social-media) provides insights into best practices for social media meta tags.
- [Google's Statement on Keywords Meta Tag](https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2009/09/google-does-not-use-keywords-meta-tag) clarifies that Google does not use the keywords meta tag for ranking websites.
- [Twitter Confirmation on Keywords Meta Tag](https://twitter.com/ChristiJOlson/status/1266415751172247552) highlights that Twitter also does not use the keywords meta tag.

These changes will streamline the meta tag implementation, reduce redundancy, and ensure alignment with current SEO practices and social media card standards. 😄
1894917537.PlainText:
Simplify Twitter Card Tags and Remove Unused Keywords Meta Tag

Enter your suggestions in details:
Issue Description
The current implementation of the metas.ts plugin in Lume includes unnecessary Twitter Card tags (twitter:*) even when OpenGraph tags (og:*) are present. Twitter provides fallback behavior for OpenGraph tags, which means that we don't need to duplicate our efforts by including both sets of tags.
Additionally, the keywords meta tag is no longer utilized by search engines for SEO purposes. Therefore, it can be safely removed to reduce output sizes and maintain consistent development practices.
Proposed Changes
Remove the twitter:title tag if og:title is present, optimizing the Twitter Card tags.
Simplify Twitter Card tags to include only twitter:site and twitter:card when og:* tags are present, eliminating redundant tags.
Consider removing the keywords meta tag as it is no longer effective for SEO, resulting in a cleaner codebase and reduced output size.
Additional Information
Twitter Card Markup Documentation provides details on Twitter's card markup requirements.
Getting Started with Twitter Cards offers guidance on implementing Twitter Cards effectively.
Social Media Meta Tags provides insights into best practices for social media meta tags.
Google's Statement on Keywords Meta Tag clarifies that Google does not use the keywords meta tag for ranking websites.
Twitter Confirmation on Keywords Meta Tag highlights that Twitter also does not use the keywords meta tag.
These changes will streamline the meta tag implementation, reduce redundancy, and ensure alignment with current SEO practices and social media card standards. 😄

Comments.Body: I'm ok about removing redundant tags but not removing the keywords meta tag. This is not only about SEO, if users want to specify the keywords tag, they should be able to do.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: So, going to create a PR!
Comments.Author: ghost

1901711198.Title: Some images not generated when using imagick and picture plugins
1901711198.Author: cgwrench
1901711198.Body: ### Version 1.18.5 ### Platform Windows ### What steps will reproduce the bug? I'm trying to use the imagick and picture plugins and have found that these don't always process images as I'd expect. If I configure the imagick plugin with the following data: ```yaml imagick: resize: 300 format: webp ``` And include the following image in a HTML page: ```html ``` I get the image `fire.webp` generated in my `_site` directory. I would expect to get two images: `fire.webp` and `/fire-300w.avif`. If I change my imagick data to an array, for example: ```yaml imagick: - resize: 300 format: webp ``` I do get the two images that I expect. ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? _No response_ ### What is the expected behavior? _No response_ ### What do you see instead? I would expect to see the superset of transforms defined in the imagick and picture plugin configuration used to generate images. ### Additional information I _think_ this is because of [the handling of page data in the picture plugin](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/plugins/picture.ts#L70C1-L74): ```typescript const imagick: Transformation[] = page.data.imagick ? Array.isArray(page.data.imagick) ? page.data.imagick : [page.data.imagick] : (page.data.imagick = []); ``` When `page.data.imagick` is an object, not an array, a new array is created containing that object, but the page data is never updated with the newly created array.
1901711198.PlainText:
Some images not generated when using imagick and picture plugins

Version
1.18.5
Platform
Windows
What steps will reproduce the bug?
I'm trying to use the imagick and picture plugins and have found that these don't always process images as I'd expect.
If I configure the imagick plugin with the following data:
And include the following image in a HTML page:
I get the image fire.webp generated in my _site directory. I would expect to get two images: fire.webp and /fire-300w.avif.
If I change my imagick data to an array, for example:
I do get the two images that I expect.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
No response
What is the expected behavior?
No response
What do you see instead?
I would expect to see the superset of transforms defined in the imagick and picture plugin configuration used to generate images.
Additional information
I think this is because of the handling of page data in the picture plugin:
When page.data.imagick is an object, not an array, a new array is created containing that object, but the page data is never updated with the newly created array.

1911004430.Title: Ordering of `source` elements generated by the picture plugin
1911004430.Author: cgwrench
1911004430.Body: ### Enter your suggestions in details: I'm trying to use the picture plugin to add support for serving multiple image formats. I've found that the order of `source` elements isn't ideal. With the following in my `_config.ts`: ```typescript site.use(picture()); site.use(imagick({ extensions: [".webp", ".jpg"] })); ``` I've added two images to a markdown file: ```markdown ``` Note that the order of the formats in the `imagick` attribute differ. I also don't have any `imagick` shared data, so the imagick plugin shouldn't do anything other that what's set up by the picture plugin. This generates the following output: ```html ``` For the first image, the JPEG format is included twice: once in a `source` element, and once in an `img` element. However, the `img` element refers to the original image, not the transformed image. As this isn't generated or copied into the build folder, this `img` returns a 404 (but the first `source` element is selected and displayed). Also, the `webp`, included in the second `source` element format will never get used, as browsers that support this format also support JPEGs, so the first `source` element is always used. The order of source elements is fine for the second image, however, this still has the issue that the `src` for the `img` element doesn't exist. Also, I think it would be preferable for this `src` to point to the generate JPEG, since this would have broader browser support. The seems picture plugin adds `source` elements for each format defined in the `imagick` attribute, but it also copies the original `img` as-is. I think it would be better for the plugin to disregard the original `img` element and select one of the formats in the `imagick` attribute to be used as the source for a new `img` element that is generated along with the `source` elements. There are a couple of things that would need a little care here: 1. If generating a new `img` element, which source format should be used? It might make sense to specify an ordered list of preferred `img` source formats (something like `["jpeg", "png", "webp", "avif"]`, perhaps). The first matching source should be used. If there are multiple sizes, then I'd suggest the largest image size is selected. 2. If generating a new `img` element, we'd loose any attributes on the original `img` element. It might make sense to introduce a `copyAttributes` option, like the one provided by the inline plugin. Rather than creating a new `img` element, an alternative approach might be to update the `src` for the original `img` element to point to a generated image. Note that what I've described above is for the case of a single image with. I think it's correct to still include a `source` for all image formats, even the one used for the `img` element, if there are multiple sizes in play. However, in the example above, there's an unnecessary `source` element. I hope this example illustrates the issue I've seen. Please let me know if you need any more information. --- As an aside, I did try to use an attribute of `imagick="webp jpg"`, i.e. without a width specified, as all I'm trying to do is generate multiple image formats, and I'd like to retain the original sizes of images. I don't think this is currently supported by the picture plugin. Is that correct?
1911004430.PlainText:
Ordering of source elements generated by the picture plugin

Enter your suggestions in details:
I'm trying to use the picture plugin to add support for serving multiple image formats. I've found that the order of source elements isn't ideal.
With the following in my _config.ts:
I've added two images to a markdown file:
Note that the order of the formats in the imagick attribute differ. I also don't have any imagick shared data, so the imagick plugin shouldn't do anything other that what's set up by the picture plugin.
This generates the following output:
For the first image, the JPEG format is included twice: once in a source element, and once in an img element. However, the img element refers to the original image, not the transformed image. As this isn't generated or copied into the build folder, this img returns a 404 (but the first source element is selected and displayed). Also, the webp, included in the second source element format will never get used, as browsers that support this format also support JPEGs, so the first source element is always used.
The order of source elements is fine for the second image, however, this still has the issue that the src for the img element doesn't exist. Also, I think it would be preferable for this src to point to the generate JPEG, since this would have broader browser support.
The seems picture plugin adds source elements for each format defined in the imagick attribute, but it also copies the original img as-is.
I think it would be better for the plugin to disregard the original img element and select one of the formats in the imagick attribute to be used as the source for a new img element that is generated along with the source elements. There are a couple of things that would need a little care here:
If generating a new img element, which source format should be used? It might make sense to specify an ordered list of preferred img source formats (something like  ["jpeg", "png", "webp", "avif"], perhaps). The first matching source should be used. If there are multiple sizes, then I'd suggest the largest image size is selected.
If generating a new img element, we'd loose any attributes on the original img element. It might make sense to introduce a copyAttributes option, like the one provided by the inline plugin.
Rather than creating a new img element, an alternative approach might be to update the src for the original img element to point to a generated image.
Note that what I've described above is for the case of a single image with. I think it's correct to still include a source for all image formats, even the one used for the img element, if there are multiple sizes in play. However, in the example above, there's an unnecessary source element.
I hope this example illustrates the issue I've seen. Please let me know if you need any more information.
As an aside, I did try to use an attribute of imagick="webp jpg", i.e. without a width specified, as all I'm trying to do is generate multiple image formats, and I'd like to retain the original sizes of images. I don't think this is currently supported by the picture plugin. Is that correct?

Comments.Body: Hi @cgwrench Thanks for the detailed explanation. Indeed, the picture plugin generates the `source` elements in the same order as the formats defined in the `imagick` attribute. It's possible to reorder the formats from the most modern format (avif, jxl) to the oldest format (jpeg) and provide an option to configure a different order if desired. The image in the img probably should be the latest image in the priority list. For example: ```php ``` The last image in the priority list should be the best supported format (jpg in this example) so it should be the image choosed for the img, to ensure old browsers not supporting the `picture` element will be able to display this image. And about the lack of width values, I'll take a look. The `@1` pixel density should be added automatically.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thanks @oscarotero, and thanks for such a quick response. In your example: ```html ``` I don't think the `source` element with `srcset="/assets/images/test2-600w.jpg"` is needed - browsers would just fallback to the same image in the `img` if that `source` element wasn't there. In the interest of minimising the markup generated, would it be possible to omit a `source` element for the source used in the `img` element? This `source` element would be needed if there were multiple sizes for the JPEG, but in the example I provided, I'm only interested in generating multiple formats, rather than multiple sizes. I appreciate that any change to this plugin would need to support both use cases though.
Comments.Author: cgwrench

Comments.Body: Okay, I'll work on that in order to have it ready for the incoming Lume 1.19
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: @cgwrench Can you try it now? Upgrade Lume to the latests development version `deno task lume upgrade --dev`
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@oscarotero That works an absolute treat. Thanks for such a quick turn around!

One think I did notice is that if I have some imagick plugin configuration in a global _data.yml file like:

```yml
imagick:
    format: [jpg, webp]
```

I get an error:

> A page will overwrite another page. Use distinct `url` values to resolve the conflict.

Is that expected? If so, no worries. I just expecting that the configurations set by the picture plugin and imagick plugins would work together, rather than clash like this.
Comments.Author: cgwrench

Comments.Body: Hmm, this is because the image is exported twice, one by the imagick plugin and other by the picture. I just fixed it by removing the duplicated transformations (two transformations with the same format/extension and suffix).

1911716640.Title: Picture plugin enhancements
1911716640.Author: cgwrench
1911716640.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

I've been testing the picture plugin, as I'm looking to migrate from Eleventy (and it's image plugin) to Lume. There are a few additional features the Eleventy's image plugin offers that I'd love to see in Lume's picture plugin[^1]:

1. `width` and `height` attributes are added to the `img` element.
2. Images aren't scaled up beyond their original dimensions.
3. It's easy to add `deocding="async"` and `loading="lazy"` attributes to the `img` element.
4. It enforces that there is an `alt` attribute set on the `img` element.
5. A hash of the image's contents is included in the filename of generated images (to support setting long cache headers).

I thought I'd raise them here to see if any of these could be incorporated into the picture plugin. I'd be happy to try and implement some of these myself, if there is an appetite to add these.

I think item 5 is covered by https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/352, and I think it makes sense to be a separate plugin rather than something purely image related. I'd love to see some progress made on this (and would be happy to try and contribute this, but would need some guidance on the right approach to take).

I think items 3 & 4 are simple enough to do with a plugin I could write myself, so no worries if these don't become part of the picture plugin.

I don't think that item 2 is possible with the picture plugin. Is this currently possible? If not, is this a feature you'd like to see with the picture plugin?

Now for item 1, again this could be done as part of plugin I could write. However, this one gave me some pause for thought. Doing this myself would mean that I've have to read the contents of the generated images to work out their dimensions. As the imagick plugin has already read and processed the images, it seems a bit wasteful to re-read them. However, this is totally doable with another plugin that runs after the picture/imagick plugins, so it's also not a deal breaker if I have to write this myself.

Is adding `width` and `height` attributes to `img` elements something that could be done with the picture plugin?

[^1]: This has partially been covered by [this discussion](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/discussions/350). Apologies for re-raising some of these points.

It enforces that there is an alt attribute set on the img element. A hash of the image's contents is included in the filename of generated images (to support setting long cache headers). I thought I'd raise them here to see if any of these could be incorporated into the picture plugin. I'd be happy to try and implement some of these myself, if there is an appetite to add these. 1920550211.Title: A separated folder for content only like `_inc` folder
1920550211.Author: ngdangtu-vn
1920550211.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Right now, content has been mixed up with logic & templates. While it has some advantage to make the content even more flexible. I still need content (and data) in a separated folder. With this feature being implemented, we can gain benefits:

1. Ability to use repo module thanks to content can be keep in a separated folder
2. From point 1, it make the content easily to be backup.
3. Other Static CMSes, for instance [decapcms.org](https://decapcms.org), can easily support without breaking anything in the logic or template.

Is it possible to do it without mess up the core?
1920550211.PlainText:
A separated folder for content only like _inc folder

Enter your suggestions in details:
Right now, content has been mixed up with logic & templates. While it has some advantage to make the content even more flexible. I still need content (and data) in a separated folder. With this feature being implemented, we can gain benefits:
Ability to use repo module thanks to content can be keep in a separated folder
From point 1, it make the content easily to be backup.
Other Static CMSes, for instance decapcms.org, can easily support without breaking anything in the logic or template.
Is it possible to do it without mess up the core?

Comments.Body:
I think Lume does the opposite: all templates, layouts, etc are in one folder (`_includes`) and the site content is out of this folder.

I can think of different ways to do that:
- Use themes. You can load any not-content file remotely, so you only have content files in the repo. The `simple_blog` theme does that: https://github.com/lumeland/theme-simple-blog/blob/main/mod.ts#L12-L48
- Use symlinks. In Lume you can create symbolic links in the src folder to other files/folders outside this folder.
- Save all your content in a folder (like `/content/`). All pages will have the url `/content/...`. Then you can create a function to remove this part (`(page) => page.data.url === page.data.url.replace("/content/", "/")`)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
About the theme, do you think it can work with Modules template engine? Because I use it a lot.

I think I will combine symlinks and rewrite urls options. It is possible for lume to read content from symlinks right? I plan to put content outside the `_src` and link it to `_src/content`.
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
> About the theme, do you think it can work with Modules template engine? Because I use it a lot.

It works for layouts (`layout: mytemplate.tmpl.js`), but not for direct imports (for example, `import foo from "./foo.ts"). although you can use import maps for that.

>  It is possible for lume to read content from symlinks right?

Yes, it was [added on version 1.18.0](https://lume.land/blog/posts/lume-1.18.0-release-notes/#symlinks-support).

BTW, In Lume 2.0 it will be possible to omit a folder name. For example, if a file is in `/content/articles/article-name.md`, you can configure the folder `content` to omit its name when generating the final urls. So the url will be `/articles/article-name/` (without the`/content` part).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Implemented in Lume 2 with the new `basename` variable.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

1922878362.Title: Allow Lume to run offline
1922878362.Author: cvburgess
1922878362.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

When trying to run `deno task lume --serve` lume dies with the following error:

```
TypeError: error sending request for url (https://cdn.deno.land/lume/meta/versions.json): error trying to connect: dns error: failed to lookup address information: nodename nor servname provided, or not known
    at async mainFetch (ext:deno_fetch/26_fetch.js:277:12)
    at async fetch (ext:deno_fetch/26_fetch.js:501:7)
    at async getLatestVersion (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.19.1/core/utils.ts:114:20)
    at async checkUpgrade (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.19.1/core/utils.ts:97:7)
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.19.1/cli/build.ts:57:5)
    at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1794:7)
    at async Command.parseCommand (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1639:14)
    at async (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.19.1/cli.ts:135:3)
```

Fetching this JSON file should fail gracefully - especially for re-builds - so devs can work from planes or trains with no issue!
1922878362.PlainText:
Allow Lume to run offline

Enter your suggestions in details:
When trying to run deno task lume --serve lume dies with the following error:
Fetching this JSON file should fail gracefully - especially for re-builds - so devs can work from planes or trains with no issue!

Comments.Body:
Good point.
This request is done once per day, just to make sure you're using the latest version of Lume. Then it's saved in the localstorage so you can run Lume offline without problems.
But I'm agree that it shouldn't fail on offline environment. I'll add a try/catch in the next version.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

1927059931.Title: tailwindcss arbitrary variants are broken
1927059931.Author: hesxenon
1927059931.Body:
### Version

1.19.1

### Platform

linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Setup a new lume project with jsx + tailwind, then create jsx layout that makes use of arbitrary variants like `[&:not(:focus)]`. 

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

every time, it's not a heisenbug :)

### What is the expected behavior?

Arbitrary variants should work

### What do you see instead?

Arbitrary variants don't work

### Additional information

I've traced this bug a bit and the reason is that when the tailwind plugin processes the `page.content` the string is already html escaped (`[&:not(:focus)]`) and thus the needed classes won't be generated.

While I can work around this by registering a processor before the tailwind plugin and just "un-escape" the content string it feels very brittle and hacky.

I'm not sure what the appropriate solution would be here but I _think_ it would make more sense to only escape the generated HTML before saving instead of adapting the tailwind plugin for this use case as this would prevent further bugs like this for other future plugins.
1927059931.PlainText:
tailwindcss arbitrary variants are broken

Version
1.19.1
Platform
linux
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Setup a new lume project with jsx + tailwind, then create jsx layout that makes use of arbitrary variants like [&:not(:focus)].
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
every time, it's not a heisenbug :)
What is the expected behavior?
Arbitrary variants should work
What do you see instead?
Arbitrary variants don't work
Additional information
I've traced this bug a bit and the reason is that when the tailwind plugin processes the page.content the string is already html escaped ([&:not(:focus)]) and thus the needed classes won't be generated.
While I can work around this by registering a processor before the tailwind plugin and just "un-escape" the content string it feels very brittle and hacky.
I'm not sure what the appropriate solution would be here but I think it would make more sense to only escape the generated HTML before saving instead of adapting the tailwind plugin for this use case as this would prevent further bugs like this for other future plugins.

Comments.Body:
Hi. I'm not a Tailwind expert. Do you mean something like this? ```jsx export default function () { return
Hello world
} ``` As I can see, the output html code is this: ```html
Hello world
``` Lume doesn't escape HTML code, maybe it's JSX? I just tried this HTML code in nunjucks and the exported code is the same: ```html
Hello world
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: damn, I wasn't sure who's responsible for escaping the generated html - so it seems the jsx plugin should be investigated? If that's not possible I guess the only fix is for the tailwind plugin to "un-escape" the content before passing it to tailwind, no?
Comments.Author: hesxenon

Comments.Body: I think it is an issue from Tailwind side. JSX does the correct thing, since in HTML the `&` must be always escaped.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
well yes and no - on one side it's tailwinds fault for building on a syntax that contains dangerous characters, on the other side this syntax is inside a string where it needn't be escaped.

So no, I don't think that we can just say tailwind has to change their syntax.
Comments.Author: hesxenon

Comments.Body: okay, I tried getting around this by passing `extensions: [".html", ".tsx"]` to the tailwindcss plugin options, to no avail :/ the respective classes sadly still aren't generated.
Comments.Author: hesxenon
oscarotero

maybe because I moved to mdx 🤔

1977287113.Title: `lume -s` does not listen on ipv6
1977287113.Author: 4censord
1977287113.Body:
### Version

v1.19.3

### Platform

Linux (Linux thearch 6.5.9-arch2-1 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 26 Oct 2023 00:52:20 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux)

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Start lume with `deno task serve`

Try to connect to http://localhost:3000 using ipv6
(e.g. `curl -6 localhost:3000`)
Run into timeout



### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Always

### What is the expected behavior?

Lume should either
* listen on ipv6 and ipv4 localhost
* Output `http://127.0.0.1:3000/ (local)` instead of `http://localhost:3000/ (local)`

### What do you see instead?

I run into a timeout trying to connect to lume using ipv6

### Additional information

_No response_
1977287113.PlainText:
lume -s does not listen on ipv6

Version
v1.19.3
Platform
Linux (Linux thearch 6.5.9-arch2-1 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 26 Oct 2023 00:52:20 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux)
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Start lume with deno task serve
Try to connect to http://localhost:3000 using ipv6
(e.g. curl -6 localhost:3000)
Run into timeout
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Always
What is the expected behavior?
Lume should either
listen on ipv6 and ipv4 localhost
Output  (local) instead of http://localhost:3000/ (local)
What do you see instead?
I run into a timeout trying to connect to lume using ipv6
Additional information
No response

Comments.Body:
I'm not familiarized with ipv6 so I have to dig into Deno server options.
Any help with this will be appreciated
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
The server is created here (https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/ecdafbf6c28cae91f1d6a573d888051ec1fb2407/core/server.ts#L81C1-L92C4)

The documentation does not mention anything about running dualstack (https://deno.land/api@v1.38.0?s=Deno.ListenOptions)

Having it listen on **only** IPv6 is as simple as
```diff
diff --git a/core/server.ts b/core/server.ts
index d52936ef..d617a220 100644
--- a/core/server.ts
+++ b/core/server.ts
@@ -83,6 +83,7 @@ export default class Server {
 
     this.#server = new HttpServer({
       port: port,
+      hostname: "[::1]",
       handler: (request, connInfo) => this.handle(request, connInfo),
     });

```
That is all i could figure out by now. I've never worked with deno before, so i wouldnt even know where to look further.
Comments.Author: 4censord

Comments.Body:
Also, i now know why i says `localhost` instead of `127.0.0.1`or similar, that is the recomendation in the docs, for windows reasons.

> Note about 0.0.0.0 While listening 0.0.0.0 works on all platforms, the browsers on Windows don't work with the address 0.0.0.0. You should show the message like server running on localhost:8080 instead of server running on 0.0.0.0:8080 if your program supports Windows.
Comments.Author: 4censord

Comments.Body: We are planning to switch to [`Deno.serve()`](https://deno.land/api@v1.38.0?s=Deno.serve) which is more performant and will replace the old API, but I didn't see any option to listen two different hostnames.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

1979693294.Title: [UX] Inline plugin should support rewriting path in `site.process()`
1979693294.Author: ngdangtu-vn
1979693294.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

This is how I setup `_config.js`

1. Install inline plugin
2. Install sass plugin
3. Add rewrite path to `_config.js`   
  ```js
site.process(['.css'], (p) => {
   if (!p.data.url) return void 0
   p.data.url = p.data.url.replace('style/', '')
})
  ```
4. Add `<link rel="stylesheet" href="/my-styles.css" inline>` to layout file

The `my-style.scss` is actually inside `/style/my-style.scss` and being rewritten to `/my-style.css`. At first I thought because the plugin ran early so I switch step 1 and step 3 position. But the result is still the same. The inline plugin requires us to input correct path before rewritten with the post-compiled file (instead scss, we go css).

This can be a bit confuse for later comeback. Is it possible to set path after rewritten only?
1979693294.PlainText:
[UX] Inline plugin should support rewriting path in site.process()

Enter your suggestions in details:
This is how I setup _config.js
Install inline plugin
Install sass plugin
Add rewrite path to _config.js
Add <link rel="stylesheet" href="/my-styles.css" inline> to layout file
The my-style.scss is actually inside /style/my-style.scss and being rewritten to /my-style.css. At first I thought because the plugin ran early so I switch step 1 and step 3 position. But the result is still the same. The inline plugin requires us to input correct path before rewritten with the post-compiled file (instead scss, we go css).
This can be a bit confuse for later comeback. Is it possible to set path after rewritten only?

Comments.Body:
This can be solved by reordering like this: 2 → 3 → 1

Not great if you want to keep all installing plugins code in 1 block but it's clear that what is happening inside the lume :?
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
The plugins/processors are executed in the same order they are registered:

1. SASS plugin: will compile the scss files and convert `/style/my-style.scss` to `/style/my-style.css`
2. Rewrite processor: converts the file to `/my-style.css`
3. Inline plugin: It can detect the`/my-style.css` from the `<link>` element and inline it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: One day, I will write a document about this one. I keep forgetting it and make a mess :p
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

2013774168.Title: (remark): Support for passing through remarkRehype configuration
2013774168.Author: marcus-crane
2013774168.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Hey there,

I've been porting my site over to Lume, which has been quite enjoyable so far. My blog makes use of [Littlefoot](https://littlefoot.js.org/) which is a small JS library for inlining footnotes.

With Lume, I'm also using Remark which is nice but by default, generates some footnotes with syntax that Littlefoot can't quite work with.

The fix for this is just to override how the footnotes are rendered but doing that requires being able to pass through some configuration to [remarkRehype](https://github.com/remarkjs/remark-rehype).

Unfortunately, it isn't possible to do this, as remarkRehype is initialised with some default settings and isn't reachable from `_config.ts` so I'd like to request that it be opened up for passthrough.

On a side note, and some of this is really down to Deno using URLs as identifiers, I was pleasantly surprised at how easy it was to [pull the remark plugin into my site's repo](https://github.com/marcus-crane/utf9k/commit/7fc7ee6b1d8152275bd9d29769cbf98847def5fb) and make this modification locally. 

The references within will break over time of course so I'd still like to request it as something officially supported but it makes me feel a little easier that it's possible to reach into Lume's bowels a little bit for small patches without having to say; fork the entire codebase (not that I would bother to get that far)

I'd be happy to try and contribute a PR as well, although I'd have to have a think about what a good API might look like. I was a bit surprised to see that none of the existing remark config made use of types too 😅 

Thanks again!
2013774168.PlainText:
(remark): Support for passing through remarkRehype configuration

Enter your suggestions in details:
Hey there,
I've been porting my site over to Lume, which has been quite enjoyable so far. My blog makes use of Littlefoot which is a small JS library for inlining footnotes.
With Lume, I'm also using Remark which is nice but by default, generates some footnotes with syntax that Littlefoot can't quite work with.
The fix for this is just to override how the footnotes are rendered but doing that requires being able to pass through some configuration to remarkRehype.
Unfortunately, it isn't possible to do this, as remarkRehype is initialised with some default settings and isn't reachable from _config.ts so I'd like to request that it be opened up for passthrough.
On a side note, and some of this is really down to Deno using URLs as identifiers, I was pleasantly surprised at how easy it was to pull the remark plugin into my site's repo and make this modification locally.
The references within will break over time of course so I'd still like to request it as something officially supported but it makes me feel a little easier that it's possible to reach into Lume's bowels a little bit for small patches without having to say; fork the entire codebase (not that I would bother to get that far)
I'd be happy to try and contribute a PR as well, although I'd have to have a think about what a good API might look like. I was a bit surprised to see that none of the existing remark config made use of types too 😅
Thanks again!

2019594424.Title: Picture/Imagick fails to display image starting with a date in the filename
2019594424.Author: enjikaka
2019594424.Body:
### Version

1.19.4

### Platform

macOS

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

- Set up imagick & picture
- Try to use and image that starts with a date and continues with a name, for example `2021-01-30-herpderp.jpg`

https://github.com/enjikaka/lume-img-error

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

100 % reproducible

### What is the expected behavior?

The image should load

### What do you see instead?

The image does not load

![localhost_3000_](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/assets/877455/206b0e85-39b8-4913-9a7f-e8ec38071893)


### Additional information

- If I change the format of the file name to `herpderp-2021-01-30.jpg` the image loads. 
- It only seems to happen when the file starts with a date? Tried `2-jordgubbe.jpg` and that works, so a number as start doesn't seem to be the trigger
- Images are referenced in HTML in `_site/` but the image files are not created
2019594424.PlainText:
Picture/Imagick fails to display image starting with a date in the filename

Version
1.19.4
Platform
macOS
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Set up imagick & picture
Try to use and image that starts with a date and continues with a name, for example 2021-01-30-herpderp.jpg
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
100 % reproducible
What is the expected behavior?
The image should load
What do you see instead?
The image does not load
Additional information
If I change the format of the file name to herpderp-2021-01-30.jpg the image loads.
It only seems to happen when the file starts with a date? Tried 2-jordgubbe.jpg and that works, so a number as start doesn't seem to be the trigger
Images are referenced in HTML in _site/ but the image files are not created

Comments.Body:
If the filename of a page starts with a date, Lume detects it and use it to the `date` page variable. More info: https://lume.land/docs/creating-pages/page-files/#page-date

So I guess what happens is Lume detects the date in `2021-01-30-herpderp.jpg` and save the file as `herpderp.jpg`. This is not a bug, but the expected behavior.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Aha. In my example repo linked there is a ”jordgubbar.jpg” so I guess the file is generated then. But the picture element doesnt reference that generated name, only the source file with date.

2022249427.Title: Cannot copy nested directory starting with underscore prefix
2022249427.Author: Maluscat
2022249427.Body:
Version: Lume v1.19.4
OS: Arch Linux

`site.copy` fails when trying to copy a directory hierarchy starting with an underscored folder:
```js
site.copy('_lib/someLibrary/css/', 'lib');
```
All files inside `_lib/someLibrary/css/` should get copied over into the `_site/lib/` folder. Instead, no files will get copied over whatsoever.

When the initial folder is not prefixed with an underscore, the sites are copied over as expected (but then, potential page files like .md or .html will get rendered, which is not the desired behavior).

As is written in the docs, copying a *single* underscored folder works just fine, for example:
```js
site.copy('_static', '.'); // Works as expected
```
2022249427.PlainText:
Cannot copy nested directory starting with underscore prefix

Version: Lume v1.19.4
OS: Arch Linux
site.copy fails when trying to copy a directory hierarchy starting with an underscored folder:
All files inside _lib/someLibrary/css/ should get copied over into the _site/lib/ folder. Instead, no files will get copied over whatsoever.
When the initial folder is not prefixed with an underscore, the sites are copied over as expected (but then, potential page files like .md or .html will get rendered, which is not the desired behavior).
As is written in the docs, copying a single underscored folder works just fine, for example:

Comments.Body:
When Lume traverses the source folder and find a folder that must be ignored (like those starting with an underscore), doesn't enter in this folder.
But you have a fair use case. I'll fix it. Thanks
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Fixed in Lume 2
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2027261721.Title: nunjucks include in for loop breaks build
2027261721.Author: allanberry
2027261721.Body:
### Version v1.19.3 ### Platform Macintosh ### What steps will reproduce the bug? Hi there. I can't build my site when I have includes (SVG) within a Nunjucks `for` loop. The problem seems to be when the `include` is within certain Nunjucks tags. For example, this builds OK ```njk {% include "test.svg" %} {% for item in ["a", "b", "c"] %}

{{item}}

{% endfor %} ``` But this does not: ```njk {% for item in ["a", "b", "c"] %} {% include "test.svg" %}

{{item}}

{% endfor %} ``` `if` tags also are broken: ```njk {% if true %} {% include "test.svg" %}

True

{% endif %} ``` but `block` tags seem to work **OK**: ```njk {% block test %} {% include "test.svg" %}

Test

{% endblock %} ``` ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? This happens each time I try and build my site with `deno task build`. It also happens when using `deno task serve`, but _it resolves itself after the first build_: if you edit the Nunjucks file, when Lume re-renders the HTML, the include is rendered OK. It will continue to render correctly until you stop the server and run either `deno task build` or `deno task serve` again. ### What is the expected behavior? The SVG should render. ### What do you see instead? The SVG silently fails to render, and the rendered HTML gets borked. ### Additional information It seems to me there's some sort of (async?) race condition, where the initial build can't find the include, but on subsequent renderings it already has the data, so it renders. Perhaps this is due to asynchronous template rendering. Nunjucks has an [`asyncEach` tag](https://mozilla.github.io/nunjucks/templating.html#asynceach) for async `for` rendering, but I tried it and it didn't work. Thanks very much, great software. Cheers.
2027261721.PlainText:
nunjucks include in for loop breaks build

Version
v1.19.3
Platform
Macintosh
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Hi there.  I can't build my site when I have includes (SVG) within a Nunjucks for loop.  The problem seems to be when the include is within certain Nunjucks tags.
For example, this builds OK
But this does not:
if tags also are broken:
but block tags seem to work OK:
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
This happens each time I try and build my site with deno task build.
It also happens when using deno task serve, but it resolves itself after the first build: if you edit the Nunjucks file, when Lume re-renders the HTML, the include is rendered OK.  It will continue to render correctly until you stop the server and run either deno task build or deno task serve again.
What is the expected behavior?
The SVG should render.
What do you see instead?
The SVG silently fails to render, and the rendered HTML gets borked.
Additional information
It seems to me there's some sort of (async?) race condition, where the initial build can't find the include, but on subsequent renderings it already has the data, so it renders.  Perhaps this is due to asynchronous template rendering.
Nunjucks has an asyncEach tag for async for rendering, but I tried it and it didn't work.
Thanks very much, great software.  Cheers.

Comments.Body:
Nunjucks is primary a sync template engine, but Lume needs to load the files in async. This is why in Lume 2 the default template engine will be [Vento](https://vento.js.org/), that works much better with async code.

For Nunjucks, the workaround is using `asyncEach / endeach` and `ifasync / endif`. If that don't work for you, make sure you don't have anything wrong because it always worked fine to me. This is an example: https://github.com/lumeland/theme-simple-blog/blob/1211528b0f4ea1364d1856e6531a53898a17e074/src/_includes/templates/post-list.njk#L2
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Thanks @oscarotero!  I appreciate your attention.  I tried `asyncEach / endeach` and it worked OK this time, I guess I did something wrong before.  :/

Looking forward to Vento!
Comments.Author: allanberry

2034011983.Title: Lightning CSS can’t be configured to use a different includes folder
2034011983.Author: paulrobertlloyd
2034011983.Body:
### Version

2.0.0

### Platform

Deno 1.38.5

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Lume options:

```js
const site = lume({
  src: "./src",
  includes: "_layouts",
});
```

LigntingCSS options:

```js
site.use(lightningCss({
  includes: "_styles",
  drafts: {
    customMedia: true,
    nesting: true,
  },
}));
```

CSS file:

`src/app.css` trying to import from `src/_styles/config/_custom-properties.css`:

```css
@import url("config/_custom-properties.css");
```

### What is the expected behavior?

CSS file gets bundled.

### What do you see instead?

Results in the following error:

```sh
Error: No result
    at napi_create_error (file:///Users/paulrobertlloyd/Library/Caches/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/napi-wasm/1.0.1/index.mjs:866:15)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/02a4df8e:1:7124758)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/02a4df8e:1:7118080)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/02a4df8e:1:7110732)
    at Object.bundle (file:///Users/paulrobertlloyd/Library/Caches/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/napi-wasm/1.0.1/index.mjs:248:19)
    at bundleAsync (file:///Users/paulrobertlloyd/Library/Caches/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/lightningcss-wasm/1.22.1/async.mjs:68:21)
    at async lightningCSSBundler (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/plugins/lightningcss.ts:143:24)
    at async Processors.run (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/core/processors.ts:31:7)
    at async Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/core/site.ts:638:5)
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/core/site.ts:506:9) {
  code: "GenericFailure",
  fileName: "/Users/paulrobertlloyd/Sites/paulrobertlloyd-v5/src/app.css",
  loc: { line: 2, column: 1 },
  data: "ResolverError"
}
```

### Additional information

Moving `config/_custom-properties.css` to `src/_layouts` folder (which is set as `site.options.includes`), the error disappears.

From a quick glance, it looks the `includes` option gets overridden by `site.options.includes` here, and perhaps `userOptions` isn’t taken? https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/plugins/lightningcss.ts?source#L57
2034011983.PlainText:
Lightning CSS can’t be configured to use a different includes folder

Version
2.0.0
Platform
Deno 1.38.5
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Lume options:
LigntingCSS options:
CSS file:
src/app.css trying to import from src/_styles/config/_custom-properties.css:
What is the expected behavior?
CSS file gets bundled.
What do you see instead?
Results in the following error:
Additional information
Moving config/_custom-properties.css to src/_layouts folder (which is set as site.options.includes), the error disappears.
From a quick glance, it looks the includes option gets overridden by site.options.includes here, and perhaps userOptions isn’t taken?

Comments.Body:
Hi. Thanks for this. It's indeed a bug here: https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/plugins/lightningcss.ts?source#L112
The plugin is getting the includes value from `site.options` instead of the plugin options.
I'll fix it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Fixed in Lume v2.0.1
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Brill, thanks for the quick fix!

2044968131.Title: Transform Images plugin should avoid upsizing images have smaller size than the resize options.
2044968131.Author: ngdangtu-vn
2044968131.Body:
### Version

2.0.1

### Platform

Linux/Deno

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. add an image worth 800 × 800
2. add another image worth 1280 × 1280
3. add resize like below:
    ```ts
    export default {
      transformImages: [
        {
          // origin
          format: 'webp',
        },
        {
          format: 'webp',
          resize: [300],
          suffix: '-xs',
        },
        {
          format: 'webp',
          resize: [900],
          suffix: '-xl',
        },
      ]
    }
    ```
5. run build script

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

always

### What is the expected behavior?

The 800 resolution image should not be upsize to 900.

### What do you see instead?

The 800 resolution image got upsize to 900.

### Additional information

I believe if we can ignore upsizing the image with smaller size can speed up the process. Moreover, there is no point to use a upsized images in web. And in case of sb really need it, we can add it as an optional feature.

Hey @oscarotero - that explains it then. I had a `posts/2023-12-01-daily.md` page with date field like this

```yaml
---
url: ./custom-post-url
---
```

I have since moved away from the `-daily` slugs in the file name to `posts/2023-12-01-custom-post-url.md` and got rid of the `url` and it works. Judging by your answer I could probably keep it in the initial format and simple append `.html` to the `url` attribute value and it would also work?
Comments.Author: MichaelHirn

Comments.Body:
You can append `.html` or `/`, depending on whether you prefer pretty urls or not. For example:

```yml
---
url: ./custom-post-url/
---
```

But in Lume 2, you have the variable `basename` that is used to change the name of the file without worrying about extensions or pretty url configurations, so it's easier to do simply:

```yml
---
basename: custom-post-url
---
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2053267049.Title: Split core functionality in `mod.ts`
2053267049.Author: scarf005
2053267049.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

currently many features are packed into [`lume/mod.ts`](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/12f8dc64b08a004c273548c9a0954bd8ec137188/mod.ts#L3-L11).

1. [`getOptionsFromCli`](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/12f8dc64b08a004c273548c9a0954bd8ec137188/mod.ts#L64) is private.
2. [`lume`](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/12f8dc64b08a004c273548c9a0954bd8ec137188/mod.ts#L29C1-L29C29) uses predefined plugins.
3. [ignorelist](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/12f8dc64b08a004c273548c9a0954bd8ec137188/mod.ts#L40-L44) is private inside function body,

it may be beneficial to extract these features into separate files. for example, it would make creating an lightweight lume function without pre-defined modules (url, json, vento, etc...) with Cli support easier.

lume uses predefined plugins.
