2058030094.Title: Image transform does not work with all caps JPG extension
2058030094.Author: RickCogley
2058030094.Body: ### Version 2 ### Platform macOS, Netlify ### What steps will reproduce the bug? If you have something like: ``` ``` .... but upload an image with an extension of JPG in upper case, the transformation does not work. ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? It's consistent, and I found out in the nwtn repo. The plugin won't render the transforms if the original is whatever.JPG. It must be whatever.jpg. ### What is the expected behavior? Users will upload whatever image format they get, if they are uploading to media folder via decapCMS for instance. They won't follow a rule like "the extension must be lowercase". ### What do you see instead? I'd like the plugin to be forgiving, and simply work, even if someone uploads whateverimage.JPG with an all caps extension. ### Additional information I didn't try but, it may be possible to set in plugin settings? `[ ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png" ]` I think the default should just allow the variants, because cameras still export files with JPG as capitals, and also there might be some dumb situation where it's something.Jpg though I've never seen it.
2058030094.PlainText:
Image transform does not work with all caps JPG extension

Version
2
Platform
macOS, Netlify
What steps will reproduce the bug?
If you have something like:
.... but upload an image with an extension of JPG in upper case, the transformation does not work.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
It's consistent, and I found out in the nwtn repo. The plugin won't render the transforms if the original is whatever.JPG. It must be whatever.jpg.
What is the expected behavior?
Users will upload whatever image format they get, if they are uploading to media folder via decapCMS for instance. They won't follow a rule like "the extension must be lowercase".
What do you see instead?
I'd like the plugin to be forgiving, and simply work, even if someone uploads whateverimage.JPG with an all caps extension.
Additional information
I didn't try but, it may be possible to set in plugin settings? [ ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png" ]  I think the default should just allow the variants, because cameras still export files with JPG as capitals, and also there might be some dumb situation where it's something.Jpg though I've never seen it.

Comments.Body:
Okay, thank you!
The extension detection must be case insensitive. I'll fix it for Lume 2.0.2
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Fixed. You can test it upgrading lume with `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: thank you for the quick work!
Comments.Author: RickCogley

Comments.Body:
Thinking of privacity and good defaults, maybe the plugin should disable access by default and only grant access to bots explicity defined. For example:

```js
site.use(robots({
  allow: ["Google", "Bing", "Yahoo", "ChatGPT"],
  paths: "/"
}));
```

This would generate this file:

```txt
User-Agent: *
Disallow: /

User-Agent: Googlebot
Allow: /

User-Agent: Bingbot
Allow: /

User-Agent: Yahoo-MMCrawler
Allow: /

User-Agent: ChatGPT-User
Allow: /
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero
kwaa

Comments.Body:
Given the myriad of possible UserAgent values, I likewise think it's best not to manage name conversions. (like `Google => Googlebot`, `ChatGPT => ChatGPT-User`)

https://darkvisitors.com/agents

2061610155.Title: Upgrading to Lume 2.0.x is breaking the build
2061610155.Author: doga
2061610155.Body:
### Version

2.0.2

### Platform

deno 1.39.1 (release, x86_64-apple-darwin), macOS 12.7.2

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

```
git clone https://github.com/doga/qworum-website.git
cd qworum-website
deno task lume upgrade
deno task serve
```

produces:

```
Task serve deno task lume -s
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run --unstable -A - "-s"
Loading config file file:///Users/[path to project]/_config.ts
TypeError: Module not found "https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.2/core.ts".
    at https://deno.land/x/lume_langdata@v1.0.9/mod.ts:10:22
    at async createSite (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.2/cli/run.ts:50:17)
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.2/cli/build.ts:31:16)
    at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1794:7)
    at async Command.parseCommand (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1639:14)
    at async https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.2/cli.ts:121:3 {
  code: "ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND"
}
```


### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

This is a deterministic bug that happens each time I try upgrading from lume v1.19.4 to lume v2.0.x.

### What is the expected behavior?

`deno task serve` and  `deno task build`  must not crash.

### What do you see instead?

`deno task serve` is crashing (and so does `deno task build`):

```
Task serve deno task lume -s
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run --unstable -A - "-s"
Loading config file file:///Users/[path to project]/_config.ts
TypeError: Module not found "https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.2/core.ts".
    at https://deno.land/x/lume_langdata@v1.0.9/mod.ts:10:22
    at async createSite (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.2/cli/run.ts:50:17)
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.2/cli/build.ts:31:16)
    at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1794:7)
    at async Command.parseCommand (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1639:14)
    at async https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.2/cli.ts:121:3 {
  code: "ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND"
}
```

### Additional information

Reverting back to lume v1.19.4 in `import_map.json` fixes the buggy behaviour.
2061610155.PlainText:
Upgrading to Lume 2.0.x is breaking the build

Version
2.0.2
Platform
deno 1.39.1 (release, x86_64-apple-darwin), macOS 12.7.2
What steps will reproduce the bug?
produces:
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
This is a deterministic bug that happens each time I try upgrading from lume v1.19.4 to lume v2.0.x.
What is the expected behavior?
deno task serve and  deno task build  must not crash.
What do you see instead?
deno task serve is crashing (and so does deno task build):
Additional information
Reverting back to lume v1.19.4 in import_map.json fixes the buggy behaviour.

Comments.Body:
In Lume 2, there's no `/core.ts` file. The types are imported from the `deno.json` file and accessible from the global `Lume` namespace (See more info here: https://lume.land/blog/posts/lume-2/#typescript-improvements).

Seems that the `lume_langdata` plugin is trying to import `https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.2/core.ts` ([here](https://deno.land/x/lume_langdata@v1.0.9/mod.ts?source#L10)), which is what's failing.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Thanks, that fixed it. 

Lume2 is looking fine from where I am standing, most notably Vento.

I've updated my _Lume_ plugins, hopefully other plugin developers are aware of this breaking Lume update:

- [https://deno.land/x/lume_navbardata](https://deno.land/x/lume_navbardata)
- [https://deno.land/x/lume_langdata](https://deno.land/x/lume_langdata)
- [https://deno.land/x/lume_cross_language_content](https://deno.land/x/lume_cross_language_content)
Comments.Author: doga

2064744262.PlainText:
"vto is not defined"

Version
v2.0.2
Platform
deno 1.39.1 (release, x86_64-apple-darwin), macOS 12.7.2
What steps will reproduce the bug?
I am trying to use the vto() Vento filter, but it seems this filter is no longer available since Vento became the default templating system ?!
Steps to reproduce
Use the vto filter in a Vento template:
... then watch the build fail:
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
This happens every time I try to use the vto() filter.
What is the expected behavior?
The build is expected to succeed.
What do you see instead?
The build aborts.
Additional information
Is there an additional step to take for activating the vto() filter since Vento became Lume's default templating system?

Comments.Body:
Vento filters use the pipe operator `|>`. This should work:

```
{{ feature |> vto(it) }}
```

The example in the documentation is from Nunjucks (maybe it's confusing, I'll fix it).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: `{{ feature |> vto(it) }}` does not fix it. I get `[object Promise]` displayed, regardless of whether the piped text contains markup to replace or not:
Comments.Author: doga

Comments.Body: I see. `vto` filter is async. You have to do `{{ feature |> await vto(it) }}`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
That fixed it, thanks.

_Sidenote:_ Vento's `vto` filter is extremely useful for i18n'ed websites, and since Vento is now officially part of Lume, I am officially retiring my [lume_cross_language_content Lume plugin](https://deno.land/x/lume_cross_language_content@v2.0.1), which was serving the same purpose. `lume_cross_language_content` wasn't really compatible with `deno task serve`, although it was working perfectly with `deno task build`.
Comments.Author: doga

2068289785.Title: JSX onclick attributes are stripped during build
2068289785.Author: brimworks
2068289785.Body:
### Version

2.0.1

### Platform

Mac

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Write a simple JSX component with an `onClick` attribute:

(`_components/Button.jsx`)
```jsx
export default ({ }, helpers) => (
<button onClick="alert('hi')">Click Me!</button>
)
```

When this component is rendered, it strips out the `onclick` HTML attribute.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Always

### What is the expected behavior?

When rendered, the `onClick` HTML attribute should remain unmolested.

### What do you see instead?

The `onClick` HTML attribute is stripped off.

### Additional information

_No response_
2068289785.PlainText:
JSX onclick attributes are stripped during build

Version
2.0.1
Platform
Mac
What steps will reproduce the bug?
Write a simple JSX component with an onClick attribute:
(_components/Button.jsx)
When this component is rendered, it strips out the onclick HTML attribute.
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Always
What is the expected behavior?
When rendered, the onClick HTML attribute should remain unmolested.
What do you see instead?
The onClick HTML attribute is stripped off.
Additional information
No response

Comments.Body:
Note that if I use `onclick` attribute name I get:

```
Warning: Invalid event handler property `onclick`. React events use the camelCase naming convention, for example `onClick`.
    at button
```

...as this was the suggestion here: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/396#issuecomment-1474031811
Comments.Author: brimworks

Comments.Body:
Seems that React cannot [render statically attributes for events](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/24440991/render-onclick-attribute-using-react-js-when-calling-rendercomponenttostaticmark).

You can use the `jsx_preact` plugin, that keeps the `onclick` attribute.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thanks for the response! Yes, switching to `jsx_preact` plugin fixes the problem. Perhaps a note about this should be added to the doc site... and maybe even make preact the default? Just some thoughts. I very much appreciate the help!
Comments.Author: brimworks

{{ comp Button }} content {{ /comp Button }} nested in {{ comp Container }}  {{ /comp }}  doesn't rendered.
Additional information
No response

I just fixed it and will be available in Lume 2.0.3.

2082102163.Author: cafour
2082102163.Body:
### Version 2.0.3 ### Platform Windows ### What steps will reproduce the bug? By following the button sample at https://lume.land/docs/core/components, I end up with `index.jsx` and `_components/button.vto` (see [lume-components.zip](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/files/13939605/lume-components.zip)). ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Always. ### What is the expected behavior? I expect `index.jsx` to produce this `index.html`: ```html

Welcome to my site.
2082102163.PlainText:
Lume components don't render in JSX pages as expected

Version
2.0.3
Platform
Windows
What steps will reproduce the bug?
By following the button sample at , I end up with index.jsx and _components/button.vto (see lume-components.zip).
How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
Always.
What is the expected behavior?
I expect index.jsx to produce this index.html:
What do you see instead?
Instead the tags in button.vto get HTML-escaped, producing:
Additional information
The same happens if I implement the button component using a js module. However, when I use JSX the button is rendered correctly.
I'm not an expert on JSX by any means. I couldn't even figure out how to make it output raw HTML without involving an unnecessary <div> wrapper with dangerouslySetInnerHTML but since the docs say that "Lume components can be used like React components if you're using the JSX plugin:", I'd assume Lume would handle the raw HTML output for me. (Or maybe the docs should be more specific and say that this works correctly only when "...the component is using the JSX plugin...".
P.S.: I really enjoy using Lume and have been doing so for months now. Thanks! :3

Comments.Body:
You're right and the documentation should clarify this. Components work well if you're using JSX components inside a text-based template engine (like JSX button inside a Vento page) or JSX components in JSX templates.

What don't work well is text-based components inside JSX template engines (like in your example, a Vento component inside a JSX page), because strings are automatically escaped in JSX.

I didn't found a good way to fix that and probably the `div` wrapper is be best solution ATM.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2090664664.Title: Date plugin: Format the date from frontmatter exactly as I specified it
2090664664.Author: brookback
2090664664.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Hi! Not sure I'm holding this wrong, but I've got a (deceivingly simple) date problem.

## Request

I want the specified date and time from my Markdown frontmatter to show in the built site. Currently, all dates and times are formatted in UTC (or whatever `TZ` is on the machine building the site).

## Use case

For the `date` field in my posts' frontmatter, I use my **current local time with time zone offset**. Like this:

```
---
date: 2024-01-18 20:44:58 +7
location: Koh Lanta, Thailand
---
```

Note the included timezone offset.

When formatting that in the templates, I want that date and time to show. It works on my machine (classic!), but when building on CI for deploying, dates are showing in UTC.

Using the format string `PPP — HH:mm`:

```
January 18th, 2024 — 13:44
```
when I want:
```
January 18th, 2024 — 20:44
```

I just want whatever I input as the formatted output 😄

I've read about setting the `TZ` var when building with `lume`, but I can't do that, since the local time for each post might vary (I might post across timezones).

Also, I can't do my own hacky date parsing and formatting, since I don't have access to the raw frontmatter record on the `Page` data: I only have the already parsed `date: Date`.

Maybe this is out of scope for Lume, but I thought I'd ask. Thanks for a never ending great job ❤️

***

**Info**

- Lume version:  v1.13.1
- OS: macOS 14.2.1

**Related:**

- https://blog.jim-nielsen.com/2023/date-and-time-in-ssg/. Known problem for other SSGs 🤷 
- https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/391. Similar issue, but not exactly the same.
2090664664.PlainText:
Date plugin: Format the date from frontmatter exactly as I specified it

Enter your suggestions in details:

Use case
For the date field in my posts' frontmatter, I use my current local time with time zone offset. Like this:
Note the included timezone offset.
When formatting that in the templates, I want that date and time to show. It works on my machine (classic!), but when building on CI for deploying, dates are showing in UTC.
Using the format string PPP — HH:mm:
when I want:
I just want whatever I input as the formatted output 😄
I've read about setting the TZ var when building with lume, but I can't do that, since the local time for each post might vary (I might post across timezones).
Also, I can't do my own hacky date parsing and formatting, since I don't have access to the raw frontmatter record on the Page data: I only have the already parsed date: Date.
Maybe this is out of scope for Lume, but I thought I'd ask. Thanks for a never ending great job ❤️

Comments.Body:
As an idea, you can preprocess the pages to change the `Date`. Let's say you create a new variable with the time zone:

```yml
date: 2024-01-18 20:44:58
timezone: Asia/Bangkok
```

And then process the pages assigning the correct timezone.

Lume 2 uses [Temporal polyfill](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@js-temporal/polyfill) (in `/deps/temporal.ts`) which has functions to work with timezones ([see docs here](https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/docs/#Temporal-ZonedDateTime)).

Let me know if you can solve it with this. If it works fine, maybe this could be a Lume plugin (Some people ask me about using different timezones in multilanguage sites).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@oscarotero Thanks for replying.

Yes, that'd work too, with the added benefit of documentation in the frontmatter.

But if not, would it be improper to suggest that Lume provides the frontmatter as a part of the `Page` interface somewhere? As I touched upon in:

> Also, I can't do my own hacky date parsing and formatting, since I don't have access to the raw frontmatter record on the Page data: I only have the already parsed date: Date.

As "raw" as possible, meaning, no `string → Date` parsing whatsoever. Just raw strings (and maybe `number | boolean` :)).

Or do you consider it leaky?
Comments.Author: brookback

Comments.Body: That's no easy. The date parsing is not performed by Lume, but by yaml parsed https://deno.land/std@0.212.0/front_matter/mod.ts
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I have only just started using Lume an  ran into the same challenge that original timezone offsets do not make it into the page's `date` object, which is to be expected with JavaScript `Date` objects.  I would have hoped the raw string would still be there somewhere, but I could not find it.

I believe it happens in: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/f973940e5d3714074cec055aed9328eb2c863815/core/utils/date.ts#L30

`front_matter` appears to just return the raw string, from what I can tell:

```js
> extractYaml(`---
date: '2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00'
---
`)
{
  frontMatter: "date: '2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00'",
  body: "",
  attrs: { date: "2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00" }
}
```

Lume is using `Temporal` internally to parse the string, but is converting it to a `Date` object, rather than returning  e.g. a `ZonedDateTime`, which would preserve all info.
```js 
Temporal.ZonedDateTime.from("2016-01-01T15:42:00+11:00[Australia/Sydney]")
```
A date with offset `"2016-01-01T15:42:00+11:00` but without timezone  info (e.g. `[Australia/Sydney]`) would not work, if I read the [docs](https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/docs/zoneddatetime.html#static-methods) correctly, though.  
So, all your date strings would need to include the timezone. Otherwise Lume would need to use `Temporal.Instant` which would also loose the parsed offset information. 

It would be great if Lume either preserved the original unparsed date (or whole plain front_matter object) in the page data or provide a hook for parsing it. A`site.prepprocess` hook already is already too late, as the date has already been parsed.
Comments.Author: kollhof

Comments.Body:
@kollhof Nice find, I didn't know Lume was using Temporal internally.

Out of curiosity, how do you plan on formatting the timezone-aware dates in your markup?

I'd love for this end-to-end flow to work:

1. Have an ISO date with timezone `date: "2016-01-01T15:42:00+11:00[Australia/Sydney]"` in the frontmatter.
2. Lume keeps this exact ISO string somewhere on the page's data object for me to do things with, if needed. Also parse it to a `Temporal.ZonedDateTime` and keep on the page data.
3. When formatting — maybe with the `date` filter — the timezone is carried through and taken into consideration when formatting according to some string or with the `Intl` API.
Comments.Author: brookback

Comments.Body:
Lume uses Temporal internally to parse the `date` if it wasn't already parsed by the yaml parser. If the date is unquoted, the YAML package (from std) parse it automatically ([here's the code](https://jsr.io/@std/yaml/1.0.2/_type/timestamp.ts)):

```yaml
date: 2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00   # parsed by @std/yaml
date: '2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00' # parsed by lume
```

What Lume does is to normalize the Date parsing to match yaml behavior in those cases in which the date is not parsed by yaml: i.e. if it's exported as a quoted date, if has a special value like `Git Created` or if it's included in the filename (`2016-01-01-example.md`).

As an idea, you can use a different named variable to store the date as you want. For example, `tz_date` to store the time zoned date (must be quoted, to prevent being parsed by YAML parser):

```yaml
tz_date: '2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00'
```

Then, you can create a preprocessor to parse the date as you want and even modify the `date` variable if you need to.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Interesting, I was not aware of the yaml parser doing that, thanks.
Comments.Author: kollhof

2091532196.Title: Parsing Exception: CSS selector query doesn't get attribute value
2091532196.Author: ngdangtu-vn
2091532196.Body: ### Version 2.0.3 ### Platform any ### What steps will reproduce the bug? The issue was submitted in discord: https://discord.com/channels/794537085641818124/1197989006789595156/1197989006789595156 1. Add `_data.yml` ```yml metas: title: "=title" description: "=summary" image: "$.feed-image" ``` 2. Add content that has this HTML element: `` ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Always. ### What is the expected behavior? The reporter expects to have the `src` set in place of meta `og:image`. ### What do you see instead? Nothing. the behaviou is correct. But it doesn't make sense for users. ### Additional information The source of problem: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/main/core/utils/data_values.ts#L26 This is an UX bug. This happens due to the convention only return the `innerHTML`. If it is a void element, nothing will returns. The design needs improve or to be removed as it makes users confused. Need further discussion about this feature.
2091532196.PlainText: Parsing Exception: CSS selector query doesn't get attribute value Version 2.0.3 Platform any What steps will reproduce the bug? The issue was submitted in discord: Add _data.yml Add content that has this HTML element: How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Always. What is the expected behavior? The reporter expects to have the src set in place of meta og:image. What do you see instead? Nothing. the behaviou is correct. But it doesn't make sense for users. Additional information The source of problem: This is an UX bug. This happens due to the convention only return the innerHTML. If it is a void element, nothing will returns. The design needs improve or to be removed as it makes users confused. Need further discussion about this feature.

Comments.Body:
So far we have 3 cases:

1. Only need children text node, for instance `h1`, `p`
2. Only need specific attribute, for instance `img` with `[src]`
3. The element provides rich data to use: `a` with `[href]` and child node

I also think about the nested element. But I don't think we need to support it. Users should point it directly to the element contains the info.


My solution could break the meaning of selector query. For example we have:

1. `$.feed-image` will always get text node
2. `$.feed-image[src]` will always get value of `src` attribute

It is pretty close to selector query syntax but act different as all query return `HTMLElement`. Another problem is some user may go very creative by adding `[src^="https:"]`... But looks on bright side, this syntax ensures a stricter query.
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
I see the problem. The solution of allowing the attribute name is a good idea, but `[src]` is confusing because it's a valid selector. I'd use a different syntax. An idea: the [`attr()` function ](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/attr). For eaxmple: 

```
$.feed-image attr(src)
```

Other solution is to make it more automatic:

- If the selector is to get an URL, get the value from `href` or `src` attribute.
- If not, get the value from the element content.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> If the selector is to get an URL, get the value from href or src attribute.

I have the 3rd case where we wouldn't know what user may need:

> 3. The element provides rich data to use: a with [href] and child node

It could be child text node from the element or it could be href.


I don't mind the `attr()` css function. Mind if I do this PR?
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body: Sure! PR is very appreciated 👍
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2132960975.Title: Merge site.copy and site.loadAssets?
2132960975.Author: oscarotero
2132960975.Body:
This is an idea that would like to discuss and hear the opinion of more people. If it's approved, it's a huge breaking change so it would be implemented in Lume 3 (that I don't have plans to work on that anytime soon).

Currently, Lume has several ways to output files to the site:

- **Pages:** They are the files intended to generate HTML files. They use template engines and layouts to render. The output path is the filename but replacing the original extension with `.html` or `/index.html` (depending on the prettyUrls configuration). For example `/dir/page.md` generates `/dir/page/index.html`.
- **Assets:** Similar to Pages but they are not rendered, only (pre)processed. The output filename is the same as the source filename. For example `/dir/file.css` generates `/dir/file.css`.
- **Static files:** They are files that are not loaded in memory but copied when all pages and assets are saved to the dest folder. They are configured with `site.copy()`.

The problem is **assets** and **static files** serves a similar purpose: output non-html files. But due static files are copies as is, they are not processed, so plugins like postcss, transform_images, sass, svgo, etc don't work with these files.

Let's see the following example:

```js
site.copy([".css"]);
site.use(postcss());
```

In this configuration, the build copy all CSS files and you may expect these files to be transformed with the `postcss` plugin. However the plugin only works with assets. I think this behavior may be confusing.

Static files exist to avoid loading in memory files for no reason. Only files that must be transformed (compiled, minified, etc) should be loaded in memory. It's a copy of the [Passthrought File Copy](https://www.11ty.dev/docs/copy/) feature of Eleventy.

But I think Lume should be more clever than this, instead of delegating to the user the decision of configuring a file to be loaded or copied, depending on the selected plugins. My idea is to merge `site.copy()` and `site.loadAssets()`. This will remove duplicated code and simplify the internal implementation. This new feature could have the generical name of Files.

Files are not loaded initially (like static files) but will be on demand. If a file is being processed, the content will be loaded automatically. For example:

```js
site.files([".css"]); // Copy files
```

```js
site.files([".css"]); // Copy files

site.process([".css"], (files) => {
  // The content of the .css files is loaded before run this processor
});
```

## How to rename files?

Due to files are not loaded initially, we cannot include data like a frontmatter with the `url` value to change the output path. We can use `_data` files to define a `url` function or change the `basename` of the folder.

We can also use preprocessors to assign data to the files without loading it (file content would be loaded only for processors, not preprocessors). For example:

```js
site.preprocess([".css"], (files) => {
  files.forEach((file) => changeUrl(file);
});
```

Static files allow to rename of the file in the `copy` function. For example `site.copy("assets", "")`. We can implement the same feature on files.

Thoughts?

Comments.Body: Hi, I certainly think it would be good to simplify this! It took me a while to figure out why things weren't working when I was trying to get a js file loaded while exploring #580. using `site.copy()` seems to override an earlier `site.loadAssets()`. This is definitely confusing. If the API surface is reduced from two to one functions, there's less opportunity for unsuspected interactions.
Comments.Author: ghost

Comments.Body: This was fixed in Lume 3. The new function `site.add()` replaces `site.copy()`, `site.loadAssets()` and `site.copyRemaining()`, making everything simpler.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

2136817712.Title: Support for removal of trailing slash in URL
2136817712.Author: pmuens
2136817712.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

First of all: Thanks a lot for working in Lume. I really enjoy it thus far.

While working on my new Blog setup which I'd love to power via Lume I ran into the problem of configuring everything so that the trailing slash in URLs is removed.

Specifically I'd love to have "clean URLs" without trailing slashes for the RSS Feed as well as Sitemap files I generate via their respective plugins. Not redirecting to a trailing-slash version would also be great.

I tried various configurations including the [`url` function](https://lume.land/docs/creating-pages/urls/#urls-as-functions) in which I returned a trailing-slash-free URL no avail.

Is there maybe already support for this and I'm missing it? Thanks a lot for looking into this!
